items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Tainted Acquittals) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This version of the The Magistrates' Courts (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Tainted Acquittals) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (1996 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st August 1997 Article 23 (Speeding offences etc: admissibility of certain evidence) of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 which makes provision for the admission in evidence, in proceedings for certain road traffic offences, of information recorded or measured by prescribed devices. These devices are prescribed in the Road Traffic Offenders (Prescribed Devices) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (S.R. 1997 No. 280).

items.title: Registration of Deeds Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 1st April 1997, revoke and replace the Registration of Deeds Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1968, and the amending Regulations set out in regulation 25.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (Prescribed Devices) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prescribes, for the purposes of Article 23 of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996, three types of speed detection devices and one device which records the position of vehicles in relation to light signals by photograph or similar means. The three speed detection devices are—

items.title: The Protection from Harassment (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order appoints 17th June 1997 for the coming into operation of Articles 2, 3, 4, 5(1) and (2), 6, 7, 8 and 9 of the Protection from Harassment (Northern Ireland) Order 1997.

items.title: Health and Safety (Petroleum-Spirit Licence Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations fix the fees payable for the grant, renewal or transfer of a petroleum-spirit licence. The new fees compared with those fixed by the Health and Safety (Petroleum-Spirit Licence Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 are as set out below:

items.title: Court Funds (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Court Funds Rules (Northern Ireland) 1979 so as to:

items.title: Lands Tribunal (Salaries) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for changes in the annual salaries payable to members of the Lands Tribunal for Northern Ireland following recommendations made in the Report of the Review Body on Senior Salaries in 1997 (HMSO CM 3540 and 3541).

items.title: Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Antrim Coast, Glens and Rathlin) Designation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Antrim Coast, Glens and Rathlin) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Slieve Gullion) Designation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Slieve Gullion) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1994.

items.title: Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Mourne Mountains and Slieve Croob) Designation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Mourne Mountains and Slieve Croob) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: The Social Security (Non-Dependant Deductions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: Environmentally Sensitive Areas (West Fermanagh and Erne Lakeland) Designation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (West Fermanagh and Erne Lakeland) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Sperrins) Designation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Sperrins) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1994.

items.title: The Road Vehicles Lighting (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”).

items.title: Salaries (Comptroller and Auditor General) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for increases in the annual salary payable to the Comptroller and Auditor General following recommendations made in the reports of the Review Body on Senior Salaries in 1996 (HMSO CM3094) and 1997 (HMSO CM3540).

items.title: The Education (1996 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation those provisions of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 which relate to children with special educational needs. Article 3, Articles 6 to 28, Schedules 1 and 2, Part I of Schedule 5 and Part I of Schedule 6 come into operation on 1st September 1997; Articles 4 and 5 come into operation on 1st September 1998.

items.title: Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment and Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contracting-out and Qualifying Earnings Factor) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations modify the application of section 44(5) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (Category A retirement pension) in relation to an earner who has earnings in a tax year beginning on or after 6th April 1997, part of which are in respect of contracted-out employment and part of which are not, or an earner in respect of whom minimum contributions are paid for part of a tax year.

items.title: Teachers' (Eligibility) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Teachers' (Eligibility) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Teachers' (Eligibility) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988. The Regulations provide for persons eligible to teach—

items.title: Companies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a new form 233 to be used by companies (including certain Part XXIII companies) in order to change their accounting reference date under Article 233 of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986. The new forms reflect amendments to Article 233 made by regulation 3 of the Companies (1986 Order) (Miscellaneous Accounting Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997, and will take effect on 22nd August 1997, the date on which those amendments come into operation.

items.title: Companies (1986 Order) (Miscellaneous Accounting Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make a number of miscellaneous amendments to the provisions of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1032 (N.I. 6) (“the 1986 Order”) concerning accounts and audit. The principal changes are as follows:

items.title: Special Educational Needs Tribunal Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the establishment of and regulate the procedure of the Special Educational Needs Tribunal established by Article 22 of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: The Social Security Administration (Fraud) (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st July 1997 the following provisions of the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997—

items.title: The Bovine Products (Production and Despatch) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake with amendments the Bovine Products (Despatch to other Member States) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996, as amended.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Proceeds of Crime (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 by adding an offence under section 108(1) of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 to the offences listed in Part I of Schedule 1 in respect of which a court of summary jurisdiction may make confiscation orders under that Order. By virtue of Article 1(2), a court of summary jurisdiction may only make a confiscation order in relation to the added offence where that offence is committed on or after the day on which this Order comes into operation.

items.title: Race Relations (Interest on Awards) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order, which comes into operation on 4th August 1997, makes special provision in relation to interest on awards and compensation orders made by industrial tribunals under the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (“the 1997 Order”).

items.title: Race Relations (Formal Investigations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations relate to notices given for the purpose of formal investigations under the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 and to non-discrimination notices issued in consequence of such investigations.

items.title: Race Relations (Questions and Replies) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 2 of this Order prescribes the forms which may be used for the purposes mentioned in Article 63(1) of the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (“the Order”). The form set out in Schedule 1 is for use where a person (“the person aggrieved”) wishes to question another (“the respondent”) whom he considers may have discriminated against him in contravention of the Order. The form set out in Schedule 2 is for use by the respondent (if he so wishes) when replying.

items.title: Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 by removing from the requirements which must be satisfied before a person can be promoted to, or appointed in, the rank of firefighter, the requirements as to maximum age, minimum and maximum height measurements and eyesight.

items.title: Foyle Area (Control of Fishing) (Revocation) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Foyle Area (Control of Fishing) Regulations 1977 and the Foyle Area (Control of Fishing) (Amendment) Regulations 1993 which controlled the netting of fish and other forms of angling in the waters of the Foyle area.

items.title: Game Birds Preservation Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prohibits, except where exemption is granted, the killing or taking of partridge, red-legged partridges and hen pheasants during the normal open season for game birds. It also restricts dealings in partridge, red-legged partridges or hen pheasants and prohibits dealings in grouse.

items.title: Education (Special Educational Needs) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations relate to the assessment of special educational needs and to statements of such needs under Part II of the Education Order 1996. Part II replaces with modifications the provisions relating to such assessments and statements in the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, and these Regulations revoke and replace with modifications the Education (Special Educational Needs) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985.

items.title: The Property (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st September 1997 the following provisions of the Property (Northern Ireland) Order 1997—

items.title: Prohibition of Right-Hand Turn (Portadown) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Prohibition of Right-Hand Turn (Portadown) Order (Northern Ireland) 1979 (S.R. 1979 No. 342).

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 to provide for the partial disregard of contributions made by an authority to a child’s maintenance under Article 15 of, and paragraph 17 of Schedule 1 to, the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 and, for income support and jobseeker’s allowance, provide for the treatment of arrears of such contributions as capital.

items.title: Registration of Clubs (Accounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations set out the requirements to which a registered club, and a club which has served notice of intention to seek registration, must conform in relation to the keeping and auditing of its records and accounts.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (1996 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 18th July 1997 Articles 16(5) and 76(4) to (6) of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996. Article 16(5) provides that nothing in Article 16 (Admissibility of records as evidence) shall enable evidence to be given with respect to any matter other than a matter of description prescribed by magistrates courts rules. Articles 76(4) to (6) enable the Lord Chancellor to make regulations with regard to the enforcement of payment of sums registered under that Article (including modifications of certain provisions of the Magistrates' Courts (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 and incidental, supplementary and consequential provision).

items.title: Contaminants in Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the enforcement and execution of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 194/96 setting maximum levels for certain contaminants in foodstuffs (“the Commission Regulation”).

items.title: The Social Security (United States of America) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 and the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 so as to give effect in Northern Ireland to the Supplementary Agreement on Social Security (set out in Schedule 1 to this Order) made between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the United States of America. The Supplementary Agreement amends the Agreement on Social Security set out in Schedule 1 to the Social Security (United States of America) Order (Northern Ireland) 1984 to take into account changes in United Kingdom legislation, in particular as relates to incapacity benefit.

items.title: One-Way Traffic (Strabane) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order amends the provisions of the One-Way Traffic (Strabane) Order (Northern Ireland) 1985.

items.title: Miscellaneous Food Additives (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Miscellaneous Food Additives Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: Education (School Information and Prospectuses) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (School Information and Prospectuses) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the 1993 Regulations”).

items.title: Legal Advice and Assistance (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981 so as to provide that a payment made under Article 15A of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 shall be disregarded in computing disposable income and capital.

items.title: Legal Aid (Assessment of Resources) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Assessment of Resources) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981 to provide that a payment made under Article 15A of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 shall be disregarded in computing disposable income and capital.

items.title: Road Traffic Fixed Penalties (Enforcement of Fines) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations are made under Part IV of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 which makes provision for a new fixed penalty scheme to be introduced in respect of road traffic offences in Northern Ireland and, in particular, for unpaid fixed penalties to be registered as sums adjudged to be paid by a conviction of a court of summary jurisdiction.

items.title: Welfare of Animals (Transport) (Remedial Action and Cost Recovery) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement as respects Northern Ireland the provisions of Article 9(2) of Council Directive 91/628 which require that the competent authority shall recover the costs of any measures which it had to have carried out because of the failure of the transporter to comply with its instructions.

items.title: Welfare of Animals (Transport) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with modifications the Welfare of Animals during Transport Order (Northern Ireland) 1994 and other animal welfare legislation, which together with that Order implemented Council Directive 91/628/EEC on the protection of animals during transport (O.J. No. L340, 11.12.91, p. 17). This Order implements that Directive as amended by Council Directive 95/29/EEC (O.J. No. L18, 30.6.95, p. 52).

items.title: Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) Orde (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the Construction Industry Training Board to the Department of Economic Development for the imposition of a further levy upon employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards the expenses of the Board.

items.title: Artificial Insemination of Cattle (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement the provisions of Council Directive 88/407/EEC (“the Directive”) as amended by Council Directives 90/120/EEC, 90/425/EEC and 93/60/EEC. For that purpose they further amend the Artificial Insemination of Cattle Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) in relation to the collection, processing, storage and transport of bovine semen. In particular they require that semen for export to a Member State of the European Community must comply with Article 3 of the Directive.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which revoke and replace the Education (Student Loans) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 as amended by the Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 come into operation on 1st August 1997.

items.title: Habitat Improvement (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Habitat Improvement Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”), which implement in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission (“the Commission”) under Article 7 of the Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92. (O.J. No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) (“the Agri-environment Regulations”) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: Specified Diseases (Notification and Movement Restrictions) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces with amendments the Specified Diseases (Notification and Movement Restrictions) Order (Northern Ireland) 1991, (as amended by the Specified Diseases (Notification and Movement Restrictions) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1995 and the Specified Diseases (Notification and Movement Restrictions) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996).

items.title: Horse Racing (Charges on Bookmakers) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 9(1) of the Horse Racing (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 provides for charges to be paid to the Department of Agriculture by persons who intend to apply for bookmakers' and bookmaking office licences.

items.title: The Social Security (Lone Parents) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”) and the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”), the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the Adjudication Regulations”).

items.title: Industrial Tribunals (1996 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Article 14 of the Industrial Tribunals (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) on the day after the Order is made.

items.title: Carriage of Dangerous Goods (Classification, Packaging and Labelling) and Use of Transportable Pressure Receptacles (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Carriage of Dangerous Goods (Classification, Packaging and Labelling) and Use of Transportable Pressure Receptacles Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) in order to correct defects in the principal Regulations.

items.title: Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 1st September 1997 revoke and replace, with amendments, the Students Awards (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 as amended by the Student Awards (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the previous Regulations”).

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations amend the Royal Ulster Constabulary Regulations 1996 (the principal regulations).

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Full-time) (Appointment and Conditions of Service) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations correct errors in the Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Full-time) (Appointment and Conditions of Service) Regulations 1996.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Appointment and Conditions of Service) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations correct an error in the Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Appointment and Conditions of Service) Regulations 1996.

items.title: Motor Hackney Carriages (Belfast) (Amendment) By-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These By-Laws revoke and replace with amendments By-Law 9 of and the Schedule to the Motor Hackney Carriages (Belfast) By-Laws made by the Council of the County Borough of Belfast on 4th June 1951.

items.title: The Noise Act 1996 (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
Certification Officer (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe and increase fees payable in respect of certain matters to the Certification Officer under the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 (“the 1992 Order”) and the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”). The fees were previously prescribed in the Certification Officer (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) as amended by the Certification Officer (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996. The 1995 Order repealed and replaced certain provisions of the 1992 Order which were referred to in the 1995 Regulations. These Regulations refer to the corresponding provisions of the 1995 Order.

items.title: Road Traffic (Fixed Penalty) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prescribes fixed penalties for fixed penalty offences. The penalty for offences involving obligatory endorsement is £40. For other fixed penalty offences which do not involve obligatory endorsement the penalty is £20.

items.title: Road Traffic (Fixed Penalty) (Offences) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order specifies the offences committed in respect of a vehicle under the various statutory provisions detailed in the Order, which are fixed penalty offences for the purposes of Part IV of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996. Any liability to conviction of these offences may be discharged by the payment of a fixed penalty which at the coming into operation of this Order is—

items.title: The Pensions (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Article 55 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 on 4th February 1997.

items.title: Road Traffic Offenders (Appropriate Driving Test) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prescribes circumstances in which a court must disqualify a person until he passes an appropriate driving test. The circumstances prescribed are where a person is disqualified under Article 35 of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (disqualification for certain offences) following conviction of an offence under any of the following provisions of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order of 1995”)—

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 by inserting a new regulation which makes it unlawful for a person to leave a vehicle or trailer where it causes unnecessary obstruction of the road.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (1996 Order) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st October 1997 the provisions of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) not yet in operation with the exception of Article 65 (Notification of court and date of trial). The 1996 Order makes provision with respect to the prosecution and punishment (including punishment without conviction) of road traffic offences in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Courses for Drink-Drive Offenders) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Articles 36 and 37 of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 give courts power to order that the period of disqualification imposed on a person convicted of a drink-drive offence is to be reduced if he completes a course approved by the Department of the Environment. Article 39 of that Order applies those provisions with modification during an experimental period. In particular, during the experimental period, orders can be made only by courts acting for designated areas.

items.title: Road Traffic Fixed Penalty (Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Part IV of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the Order” makes provision for a new system of fixed penalties which comes into force on 1st October 1997.

items.title: The Motor Cars (Silence Zones) (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke five Statutory Rules which prohibited the use of a motor car horn between certain hours in six specified areas of Northern Ireland. The Rules are obsolete as there is a general prohibition, subject to certain exceptions, of the use of an audible warning instrument on a vehicle between 11.30 p.m. and 7.00 a.m. This prohibition is contained in regulation 96 of the Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 5) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 1 to the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 so as to make service charges for general counselling and support eligible for housing benefit where the claimant occupies supported accommodation (as defined) (regulation 2(1)).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 6) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the amending Regulations”) so as to omit amendments to regulation 11 of the Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 461) which would have had the effect from 6th October 1997 of further restricting the maximum rent on which housing benefit may be paid in the case of single claimants aged 25 but under 60. These Regulations also make provision with respect to cases to whom the amending Regulations are not to apply.

items.title: The Fertilisers (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Fertilisers Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) and implement as respects Northern Ireland Commission Directive 96/28/EC (O.J. No. L140, 13.6.96, p. 30) adapting to technical progress Council Directive 76/116/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the member States relating to fertilisers.

items.title: Bovines and Bovine Products (Despatch Prohibition and Production Restriction) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations give new effect to Commission Decision 96/239/EC on emergency measures to protect against bovine spongiform encephalopathy, as amended by Commission Decision 96/362/EC, in relation to the despatch to third countries and other member States of bovine animals and embryos and meat and other products from bovine animals.

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) (Procedure) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1995 which make provision as to the procedure to be followed where a complaint or dispute relating to an occupational or personal pension scheme is referred to the Pensions Ombudsman under Part X of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 (“the Act”).

items.title: General Medical Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, those provisions of the General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 (the 1973 Regulations) which relate to general medical services. The provisions of the 1973 Regulations which relate to pharmaceutical services are consolidated, with amendments, in the General Pharmaceutical Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997. These Regulations therefore regulate the terms on which general medical services are provided under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: Pharmaceutical Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, those provisions of the Health and Personal Social Services (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 (“the 1973 Regulations”) which relate to pharmaceutical services. The provisions of the 1973 Regulations which relate to General Medical Services are consolidated with amendments, in the Health and Personal Social Services (General Medical Services) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997. These Regulations now regulate the terms on which pharmaceutical services are provided under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: Charges for Drugs and Appliances Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Regulations which provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances (other than dental and optical appliances) supplied under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 in consequence of certain provisions of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”). The principal changes concern the re-testing of drivers following disqualification.

items.title: Employment (Miscellaneous Provisions) (1990 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 24th August 1997 all the provisions of the Employment (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 (“the 1990 Order”) which are not already in operation, these being provisions for the repeal of section 117A of the Factories Act (Northern Ireland) 1965 (which relates to the duty of a factory occupier to give notice of employment of a young person to the local office of the Training and Employment Agency) and enactments related to it.

items.title: Traffic Signs Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
Health and Safety (Young Persons) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
Further Education Teachers' (Eligibility) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Further Education General Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 and the Further Education General Amending Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1974. The Regulations provide for teachers in institutions of further education—

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations replace the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 (“the 1991 Regulations”) which are, subject to savings, now revoked. The Regulations supplement the changes introduced by the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) to Part X of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 (“the Act”). They also consolidate and amend those provisions of the 1991 Regulations which remain relevant. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, these Regulations make the following changes of substance—

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) by bringing registered medical practitioners within the definition of an “employing authority” and thereby enabling persons employed by them to join the Health and Personal Social Services Superannuation Scheme (though periods of work which do not relate to the provision of general medical services in accordance with arrangements made under Article 56 of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) 1972 will not count as superannuable service).

items.title: Departments (Transfer of Functions) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order transfers to the Department of the Environment the functions of the Department of Economic Development under Article 17 of the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 which relate to the payment of grants for the improvement of energy efficiency in certain dwellings.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support. The rate is increased from 7·20 per cent. to 7·57 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, the Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 which made a previous amendment to the standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: Transport of Animals and Poultry (Cleansing and Disinfection) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order requires the cleansing and disinfection of any means of transport in which animals (Article 3), livestock (Article 4) or poultry (Article 7) are transported and provides for exemptions in certain circumstances (Articles 5 and 7(2)). The Order also makes provision for the cleansing and disinfection of lairages (Article 6) and any means of transport used for the transport of carcases or dung (Article 4(2)).

items.title: Plant Health (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Plant Health Order (Northern Ireland) 1993. The Order implements as respects Northern Ireland Commission Directive 97/14/EC removing the prohibition on the import of ware potatoes from Algeria into the Community (O.J. No. L.87, 02.04.97, p. 17).

items.title: Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
Education (Assessment Arrangements for Key Stages 1 and 2) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Education (Assessment Arrangements for Key Stages 1 and 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 so as to provide that a claimant is treated as possessing certain child benefit and that certain child benefit is disregarded in the calculation of income other than earnings in certain circumstances (regulations 3 and 8(b)).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Requirement to obtain Audited Accounts and a Statement from the Auditor) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of documents which the trustees or managers of an occupational pension scheme must obtain.

items.title: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (1997 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of provisions of the Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 as follows—

items.title: Explosives (Fireworks) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the possession, purchase, sale or use of certain categories of fireworks, except under licence issued by the Secretary of State, to whom application shall be made in writing. A licence fee will be payable. (Regulations 4, 5, 6 and 9). Fireworks exempt from the general prohibition are described (regulation 8).

items.title: Seed Potatoes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Seed Potatoes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981. The Regulations implement as respects Northern Ireland Council Directive 96/72/EC and provide for the substitution of the letters “EC” for the letters “EEC” on official labels for seed potatoes but provide that labels printed before the commencement of the Regulations bearing the abbreviation “EEC” may continue to be used until 31st December 2001.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime (Enforcement of Confiscation Orders made in England and Wales or Scotland) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement in Northern Ireland of confiscation orders made in England and Wales under Part VI of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 and the Drug Trafficking Act 1994 and in Scotland under the Proceeds of Crime (Scotland) Act 1995.

items.title: Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: The Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) as amended by the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) of 1989, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1995 and 1996 prescribe the amount of payments to be made under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 (“the Order”), as amended by the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1985, the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 and the Social Security (Consequential Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, to persons disabled by a disease to which the Order applies (namely pneumoconiosis, byssinosis, diffuse mesothelioma, primary carcinoma of the lung (where accompanied by asbestosis or bilateral diffuse pleural thickening) and bilateral diffuse pleural thickening) or to dependants of persons who immediately before they died were disabled by such a disease. All such persons should satisfy the conditions of entitlement prescribed in Article 3 (Payments to disabled persons) or, as the case may be, Article 4 (Payments to dependants of disabled persons) of the Order.

items.title: The Airports (Designation) (Facilities for Consultation) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Under Article 20 of the Airports (Northern Ireland) Order 1994 the operator of an airport to which the Article applies shall provide adequate facilities for consultation with respect to any matter concerning the management or administration of the airport for users of the airport, the General Consumer Council for Northern Ireland, any appropriate district council and any other organisation representing local interests.

items.title: The Road Traffic Regulation (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st October 1997 the provisions of the Road Traffic Regulation (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 which repeal certain provisions of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 and the said Order of 1997 as a consequence of the coming into operation of the provisions of the Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: Seeds (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Regulations controlling the marketing of cereal seeds, fodder plant seeds, beet seeds, oil and fibre plant seeds, and vegetable seeds (“seed regulations”) provide that, with certain exceptions, seeds of these categories may only be marketed if they are derived from crops which on inspection are found to meet certain standards and if upon subsequent testing samples of the seed also meet certain standards.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments and Adjudication) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 in the following respects—

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations further amend the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations 1988 (the principal regulations).

items.title: Food (Pistachios from Iran) (Emergency Control) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order implements the Commission Decision of 8th September 1997 on the temporary suspension of imports of pistachios and certain products derived from pistachios originating in or consigned from Iran which is not yet published in the Official Journal.

items.title: Education (Assessment Arrangements for Key Stage 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order, which revokes and replaces the Education (Assessment Arrangements for Key Stage 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996, prescribes the arrangements for the assessment of pupils in English, mathematics and science in key stage 3. The Order comes into operation on 20th October 1997.

items.title: Companies (Revision of Defective Accounts and Report) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Article 253(3) and (4) of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 make certain amendments to the Companies (Revision of Defective Accounts and Report) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 (S.R. 1991 No. 268). In particular—

items.title: Fisheries Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Byelaws revoke and replace with amendments the Fisheries Consolidated and Amendment Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 1989.

items.title: The Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 to provide that:

items.title: The Social Security Commissioners Procedure (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Commissioners Procedure Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 to provide that any application for leave to appeal or any appeal to a Commissioner from a decision of a social security appeal tribunal, a disability appeal tribunal, a medical appeal tribunal shall be accompanied by a copy of the tribunal’s statement of the reasons for its decision and of its findings on questions of fact material thereto.

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This version of the Magistrates' Courts (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (“the Order”) provides for a reformed scheme to enable the Department to recover amounts equal to certain social security benefits from persons making compensation payments to or in respect of persons who have suffered an accident, injury or disease.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations further amend the Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations 1988 (the principal regulations).

items.title: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 provides for a reformed scheme to enable the Department to recover amounts equal to certain social security benefits from persons making compensation payments to or in respect of persons who have suffered an accident, injury or disease.

items.title: Councillors (Travelling and Subsistence Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: The regulations amend the Local Government (Travelling and Subsistence) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 by enabling councils to pay travelling and subsistence allowances to councillors and committee members of district councils where the payments do not exceed the amount or rates determined by the Department of the Environment.

items.title: Processed Cereal-based Foods and Baby Foods for Infants and Young Children Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Directive 96/5/EC (O.J. No. L49, 28.2.96, p. 17) on processed cereal-based foods and baby foods for infants and young children.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: Companies (1986 Order) (Accounts of Small and Medium-sized Companies and Minor Accounting Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
The Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments) (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 by providing for a procedure to be operated when a weather station specified in Schedule 1 is unable to provide temperature information in respect of a certain day or period (regulation 2).

items.title: Primary Schools (Admissions Criteria) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 16 of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 requires the Board of Governors of each grant-aided school to draw up the criteria to be applied in selecting children for admission to the school. It also enables the Department of Education to make Regulations requiring the criteria to include, or not to include, such matters or matters of such description as are specified in the Regulations.

items.title: Secondary Schools (Admissions Criteria) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Secondary Schools (Admissions Criteria) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995.

items.title: Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for the payment by the Department of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital.

items.title: The National Board for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting for Northern Ireland (Constitution and Administration) Amendment Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
Police (Property) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations revoke the Police (Disposal of Property) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1974. They substantially re-enact the provisions in those regulations relating to sale and disposal of property in the possession of the police to which the Police (Property) Act 1897 applies, and make corresponding provision for property in the hands of the police by virtue of Article 11 of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1994. The regulations also make new provision for the retention of property by the police in certain circumstances.

items.title: The Social Security Administration (Fraud) (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Article 10 of the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (which concerns the obtaining of information from landlords and agents) on 8th October 1997. It also provides for the coming into operation of Article 15 of that Order (which concerns recovery of overpaid housing benefit) for the purposes of making regulations on 8th October 1997 and for all other purposes on 3rd November 1997.

items.title: The Industrial Training (Construction Board) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 1 to the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order (Northern Ireland) 1964 (the 1964 Order) by deleting references to electrical work and providing that those establishments involved mainly in electrical installation, maintenance and repair work shall not be included in the activities of the construction industry for the purposes of the 1964 Order.

items.title: Foods Intended for Use in Energy Restricted Diets for Weight Reduction Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Directive 96/8/EC on food intended for use in energy-restricted diets for weight reduction.

items.title: Eggs (Marketing Standards) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Eggs (Marketing Standards) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal regulations”) to extend the enforcement responsibilities of district councils to include the sale of eggs by retailers. In addition, they make provision for the enforcement and execution of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1511/96 amending Regulation (EEC) No. 1274/91 introducing detailed rules for implementing Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1907/90 on certain marketing standards for eggs.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 7) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 so as to—

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Information from Landlords and Agents) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement provisions of the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/1182 (N.I. 11)) (“the Order”). Regulation 2 gives the Northern Ireland Housing Executive in relation to the administration of housing benefit and any person exercising such functions for them a power to require certain landlords and their agents to provide information as to their interests in residential property, including that of any associated bodies (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Recovery of Overpayments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement provisions of the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/1182 (N.I. 11)) (“the Order”) in relation to the recovery of overpayments of housing benefit.

items.title: Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations—revoke and re-enact with modifications, in a consolidated form, the Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 (S.R. 1986 No. 247) and certain provisions of the Offshore Installations (Inspectors and Casualties) Regulations 1973 (S.I. 1973/1842) and the Submarine Pipe-lines (Inspectors etc.) Regulations 1977 (S.I. 1977/835) (see paragraph 2), andrepeal a provision of the Quarries (Northern Ireland) Order 1983 (S.I. 1983/150 (N.I. 4)) and make a consequential amendment to the Quarries (Returns and Records) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1963 (S.R. & O. (N.I.) 1963 No. 41) (see paragraph 3).

items.title: Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 47 of the Housing (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 provides, inter alia, that where an application to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive for a grant is accompanied by an owner-occupier certificate or a tenant’s certificate (as defined in Article 44(2) and (3)), then, if the financial resources of the applicant exceed the applicable amount determined in accordance with regulations, the amount of the grant shall be reduced in accordance with those regulations.

items.title: The Commissioner for Complaints (1997 Amendment Order) (Commencement ) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation the whole of the Commissioner for Complaints (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (“the Order”) on 1st December 1997. The Order also brings certain actions, begun before 1st December 1997 and continuing on or after that date, within the scope of the Commissioner for Complaints (Northern Ireland) Order 1996, as amended by this Order

items.title: Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Optical Charges and Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with eye sight tests and the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) by providing that from 28th February 1997 in relation to any employer engaged wholly or mainly in electrical construction, installation, maintenance or repair work nothing in the 1996 Order:

items.title: The Welfare Foods (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Welfare Foods Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 to enable local Education and Library Board controlled premises to participate in the Welfare Milk Scheme.

items.title: Dogs (Licensing and Identification) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Dogs (Licensing and Identification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1983 by setting out, for the period 1st January 1998 up to and including 31st December 2001, the shape, dimensions and colour of licence identification discs issued by district councils.

items.title: Eel Fishing (Licence Duties) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations increase the licence duties payable from 1st January 1998 to the Fisheries Conservancy Board for Northern Ireland in respect of licences for the use of fishing engines for the taking of eels by the amounts set out below.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations amend the Royal Ulster Constabulary Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 1993.

items.title: Transport of Animals and Poultry (Cleansing and Disinfection) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Transport of Animals and Poultry (Cleansing and Disinfection) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997. The Order requires the cleansing and disinfection of any means of transport in which animals (Article 3), livestock (Article 4) or poultry (Article 7) are transported and provides for exemptions in certain circumstances (Articles 5 and 7(2)). The Order also makes provision for the cleansing and disinfection of lairages (Article 6) and any means of transport used for the transport of carcases or dung (Article 4(2)).

items.title: Motorways Traffic (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: By virtue of the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 542), separate driving tests were introduced in respect of motor cars with trailers (category B+E), trucks and vans of between 3·5 tonnes and 7·5 tonnes maximum authorised mass (sub-categories C1 and C1+E) and buses having more than 8 but not more than 16 passenger seats whether or not they carry passengers for hire or reward (sub-categories D1 and D1+E). Provisional licences to drive these classes of vehicle may, however, only be issued to persons already holding at least a full licence to drive motor cars (category B). Those regulations thereby implement that part of Council Directive 91/439/EEC of 29th July 1991 (O.J. No. L237, 24.8.91, p. 1) relating to driving tests.

items.title: Control of Pesticides (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Pesticides Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 414), as amended (S.R. 1991 No. 203), (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations have been made for the purpose of controlling pesticides in implementation of Part III of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 and require the advertisement, sale, supply, storage and use in Northern Ireland of any pesticide product falling within the scope of the principal Regulations to be carried out in accordance with an approval granted by the Department of Agriculture for Northern Ireland (“the Department”) in relation to that product and in accordance with consents made by the Department (“the consents”).

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Basic Conditions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations supplement the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 371) as amended (S.R. 1996 No. 456) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Northern Ireland Council Directive No. 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market, as amended. The principal Regulations require that plant protection products shall not be placed on the market or used within Northern Ireland unless they have been approved by the Department of Agriculture, for Northern Ireland (“the Department”) and make provision concerning the process of application for, evaluation and granting of approvals, which may be given subject to conditions and the packaging and labelling of approved plant protection products.

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 371), as amended by S.R. 1996 No. 456, (“the principal Regulations”) which implement as respects Northern Ireland Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”), as amended by Commission Directive 93/71/EEC, Commission Directive 94/37/EEC, Commission Directive 94/79/EC, Commission Directive 95/35/EC and Commission Directive 95/36/EC, each of which amends Annexes II and III to the Directive.

items.title: The Social Fund (Maternity and Funeral Expenses) (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund (Maternity and Funeral Expenses) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) by—

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Payments to Employers) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Occupational Pension Schemes (Payments to Employers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Carriage of Explosives by Road Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations impose requirements and prohibitions with regard to the carriage of explosives by road. They implement Council Directive 94/55/EC (O.J. No. L319, 12.12.94, p. 7) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States with regard to the transport of dangerous goods by road, in so far as the Directive concerns explosives. This Directive seeks to apply the European Agreement Concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road signed at Geneva on 30th September 1957, as amended (“ADR”) (1995 edition: ISBN 92 1 139043 5).

items.title: The Carriage of Explosives by Road (Driver Training) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support. The rate is increased from 7·57 per cent. to 7·97 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, the Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 which made a previous amendment to the standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: Arable Area Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Arable Area Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995. As respects Northern Ireland the Regulations implement and supplement provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1765/92 (“the Council Regulation”), which establishes a support system for producers of certain arable crops, and the Commission Regulations made under it, namely Commission Regulations (EC) No. 762/94, 658/96 (which repeals and replaces Commission Regulations (EEC) Nos. 2294/92, 2295/92 and 2780/92) and 1586/97 (which repeals and replaces Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 334/93).

items.title: Potatoes Originating in the Netherlands (Notification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 8th December 1997, impose certain requirements in respect of potatoes grown in 1997 in the Netherlands in accordance with Commission Decision 95/506/EC authorising Member States to take additional measures against the dissemination of Pseudomonas solanacearum (Smith) Smith as regards the Kingdom of the Netherlands (O.J. No. L.291, 6.12.95, p. 48), as last amended by Commission Decision 97/649/EC (O.J. No. L.274, 7.10.97, p. 14).

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Requisite Surviving Spouses' Benefits etc) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides that, except in circumstances prescribed by regulations made under the Pensions Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993, a surviving spouse shall be entitled to a pension on the death of a member of a judicial pension scheme providing the marriage took place before the member retired. This is to comply with the provisions of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 as amended by the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 which provide that a pension scheme must entitle a surviving spouse to a pension in order for a contracting-out certificate to be granted in relation to that scheme.

items.title: The Social Security Administration (Fraud) (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Article 18 of the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997, which concerns a requirement to state national insurance number, for the purposes of making regulations on 7th November 1997 and for all other purposes on 1st December 1997.

items.title: Building (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (revoked)
items.description: These regulations amend the Building Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (“the principal regulations”). They come into operation on 1st January 1998.

items.title: Building (Prescribed Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations revoke and replace the Building (Prescribed Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1982 as amended.

items.title: The Social Security (National Insurance Number Information: Exemption) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Guardian’s Allowances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1975, the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1976, the Social Security (Widow’s Benefit and Retirement Pensions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979, the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1984, the Social Security (Maternity Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 by ensuring that where a linked period commenced before 6th October 1996, any day of unemployment which forms part of a period of interruption of employment shall be treated as a jobseeking period if that period ends within 8 weeks of the commencement of the linked period (regulation 2).

items.title: The Sheep Annual Premium (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Sheep Annual Premium Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 as amended to implement, as respects Northern Ireland, new paragraph 1(a)(3) of Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 2700/93 (O.J. No. L245 , 1.10. 93, p. 99), inserted by Article 1 of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1526/96 (O.J. No. L190, 31.7.96, p. 21). The Regulations require sheep producers to demonstrate the location of the holdings on which these sheep are kept by submitting the area aid application required by Article 6 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3508/92).

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
The Surface Waters (Fishlife) (Classification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a system for classifying the quality of freshwaters which need protection or improvement in order to support fish life.

items.title: The Surface Waters (Shellfish) (Classification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a system for classifying the quality of coastal and brackish waters which need protection or improvement in order to support shellfish life and growth.

items.title: Rates Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Rates (Amendment) Regulations (N.I.) 1976 by making new provisions prescribing the periods within which district rates for any financial year must be made and notified to the Department of Finance and Personnel. The Regulations make one change of substance. They extend the period within which a district council must make the district rate for the year ending 31st March 1998 so that it ends on 28th February 1997 rather than 15th February of that year.

items.title: Food Safety (Enforcement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the Food Safety (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 to be enforced and executed by the Department of Agriculture in the case of certain premises and activities.

items.title: Fresh Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations give effect to Council Directive 91/497/EEC (OJ No. L.268, 24.9.91, p. 69) which amends and updates Directive 64/433/EEC on health problems affecting intra-Community trade in fresh meat to extend it to the production and marketing of fresh meat, and in part to Council Directive 91/495/EEC (O.J. No. L.268, 24.9.91, p. 41) concerning public health and animal health problems affecting the production and the placing on the market of farmed game meat and Council Directive 92/45/EEC (O.J. No. L.268, 16.6.92, p. 35) on public health and animal health problems relating to the killing of wild game and the placing on the market of wild game meat.

items.title: Meat Products (Hygiene) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 92/5/EEC (O.J .No. L57, 2.3.92, p. 1) (“the Directive”) amending and updating Directive 77/99/EEC (O.J. No. L.26, 31.1.77, p. 85) on health problems affecting intra-Community trade in meat products and amending Directive 64/433/EEC (OJ No. 121, 29.7.64, (OJ/SE l963-64 p.185)), as amended by Article 14(3) of Council Directive 92/45/EEC (O.J. No. L.268, 14.9.92, p. 35) read with Commission Decision 94/383/EC (O.J. No. L.174, 8.7.94, p. 33). The Regulations relate to States (other than Iceland) in the European Economic Area. This is by reason of the EEA Agreement (at O.J. L.1, 3.1.94) and Decision No. 7/94 of the EEA Joint Committee (see Annex 2 at O.J. L.160/13, 28.6.94).

items.title: Minced Meat and Meat Preparations (Hygiene) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 94/65/EC (O.J. No. L.368, 31.12.94, p.10) laying down the requirements for the production and placing on the market of minced meat and meat preparations.

items.title: Wild Game Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations give effect, in part, to Council Directive 92/45/EEC (O.J. No. L.268, 14.9.92, p. 35) on public health and animal health problems relating to the killing of wild game and the placing on the market of wild game meat.

items.title: Court Funds (Amendment No. 3) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Court Funds Rules (Northern Ireland) 1979 so as to increase the rate of interest payable—

items.title: Imported Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations contain measures relating to the control of certain types of food imported into Northern Ireland which are not in free circulation within the European Community. They replace the general provisions of the Imported Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 with a new set of provisions for all food other than exempt products of animal origin. The Regulations also contain amendments to the Food Safety (General Food Hygiene) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 which implement Commission Directive 96/3/EC which grants a derogation from certain provisions of Council Directive 93/43/EEC on the hygiene of foodstuffs (O.J. No. L.175, 19.7.93, p. 1).

items.title: Temporary Speed Limit (Lisnevenagh Road, Route A26, Kells) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prohibits in the interests of safety the driving of motor vehicles at more than 40 miles per hour on Lisnevenagh Road, Route A26, Kells, from its junction with Oldwood Road, to its junction with Valley Road, for a period of 4 months commencing on 1st March 1997.

items.title: Rates (Making and Levying of Different Rates) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: A new valuation list comes into effect on 1st April 1997 for purposes of Part III of the Rates (N.I.) Order 1977. That new valuation list contains general revaluations of all properties in Northern Ireland other than dwelling houses, private garages and private storage premises. These properties (“domestic properties”) are not subject of the general revaluation.

items.title: Companies (1986 Order) (Audit Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
Companies (1986 Order) (Directors' Report) (Statement of Payment Practice) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Article 242 of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 (“the 1986 Order”) and Part VI of Schedule 7 to the 1986 Order.

items.title: Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland 1989 (“the 1989 Order”) which specifies a number of persons or bodies as public authorities for certain purposes under Part II of the Fair Employment (Northern Ireland) Act 1989 (“the Act of 1989”) and provides for the persons who are to be treated for such purposes as the employees of some of those authorities.

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 371), as amended by S.R. 1996 No. 456 and S.R. 1997 No. 471 (“the principal Regulations”) which implement as respects Northern Ireland Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”), as amended by Commission Directive 93/71/EEC, Commission Directive 94/37/EEC, Commission Directive 94/79/EC, Commission Directive 95/35/EC, Commission Directive 95/36/EC, Commission Directive 96/12/EC and Commission Directive 96/46/EC each of which amends Annexes II and III to the Directive.

items.title: The Social Security Administration (Fraud) (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
