items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Article 14 of, and paragraph 7(a) of Schedule 1 to, the Social Security Administration (Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997, which concerns a penalty as an alternative to prosecution, for the purposes of making regulations on 21st November 1997 and for all other purposes on 18th December 1997. It also provides for the coming into operation of consequential amendments in paragraphs 5 and 7(d) of Schedule 1 to that Order on 18th December 1997.

items.title: Fisheries (Licence Duties) Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Byelaws amend the Fisheries Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 1997 to increase the licence duties payable from 1st January 1998 for fishing with rod and line and hand line for salmon and freshwater fish and for commercial fishing engines used for the taking of salmon and freshwater fish other than eels together with the fee payable for a licence which authorises the holder to buy and sell salmon, trout and eels. The new licence fee rates are set out below:—

items.title: Employer’s Liability (Compulsory Insurance) Exemption (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Employer’s Liability (Compulsory Insurance) Exemption Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1975 (S.R. 1975 No. 230) (“the 1975 Regulations”). The 1975 Regulations specify the employers who are exempted from the requirements of Part III of the Employer’s Liability (Defective Equipment and Compulsory Insurance) (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (S.I. 1972/963 (N.I. 6)) (“the 1972 Order”) to insure and maintain insurance against liability for personal injury suffered by their employees and arising out of and in the course of their employment. The exemptions specified in the 1975 Regulations are additional to those contained in Article 7 of the 1972 Order.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 1997/2815) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies appropriate revaluation percentages. The percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 80 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993.

items.title: The Social Security (Penalty Notice) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Section 109A of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the Administration Act”), provides for a person who has caused an overpayment of social security benefit or housing benefit, to pay a penalty in lieu of criminal proceedings. In this connection the Department of Health and Social Services for Northern Ireland, the Department of the Environment for Northern Ireland or the Northern Ireland Housing Executive may give the person a notice about the agreement to pay a penalty. These Regulations require such a notice to set out certain additional information relating to the operation of these provisions.

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 so that the maximum deduction for relevant child care charges in respect of a claimant’s family shall be £60 per week where charges are paid, in respect of one child only or £100 per week where charges are paid for more than one child.

items.title: The Insolvent Companies (Reports on Conduct of Directors) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Insolvent Companies (Reports on Conduct of Directors) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1991 (S.R. 1991 No. 368) (“the 1991 Rules”) subject to transitional and saving provisions.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989.

items.title: The Crown Court (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Confidentiality) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for the practice and procedure to be followed in the Crown Court in relation to—

items.title: The Waste Collection and Disposal (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends Schedule 6 to the Waste Collection and Disposal Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 by providing that a disposal licence under the Pollution Control and Local Government (Northern Ireland) Order 1978 is not required where waste mammalian protein is deposited in accordance with the new paragraph 16A or plant or equivalent is used for dealing with waste mammalian protein in accordance with the new paragraph 23.

items.title: The Crown Court (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Disclosure) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for the practice and procedure to be followed in the Crown Court in relation to—

items.title: Deseasonalisation Premium (Protection of Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 4c of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 805/68 (O.J. No. L148, 28.6.68, p. 24 (O.J./SE 1968 (1) p. 187)) (inserted by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2066/92 (O.J. No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 49)) as amended provides for the payment to beef and veal producers of a deseasonalisation premium. Detailed rules for the administration of the premium are laid down in Articles 1, 19 to 22, 42 to 45, and 54 to 61 of the Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3886/92 (O.J. No. L391, 21.12.92, p. 20) as amended.

items.title: The Local Government (Competition in Functional Work) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: The Local Government (Competition in Functional Work) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 describe for the purposes of Article 7(3) of the Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 (“the Order”) the nature of the functional work (as defined in Article 3(1)) which the councils specified in the schedule may not carry out on or after the dates specified in the schedule unless certain conditions (designed to ensure competition) are fulfilled (regulation 2).

items.title: Criminal Justice (1996 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st January 1998 the following provisions of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996—

items.title: The Health Services (Primary Care) (1997 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This order brings into operation on 12th January 1998 those provisions of the Health Services (Primary Care) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997, specified in the Schedule, which are necessary for the preparation of proposals for pilot schemes and the funding of preparatory work for those schemes, and for the supply of certain optical or pharmaceutical services under HSS contracts.

items.title: Section 2(1) of the Petroleum Act 1987 (Modification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make a modification to section 2(1)(a) of the Petroleum Act 1987 (“the 1987 Act”) which appears to the Department of Economic Development to be necessary or expedient as a result of the repeal by the Offshore Safety (Repeals and Modifications) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 of section 2 of the Mineral Workings (Offshore Installations) Act 1971 (“the 1971 Act”) (registration of offshore installations).

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts Rules (Northern Ireland 1984 (“the principal Rules”) in consequence of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the Order”). The Order provides a sentencing framework which lays down procedural requirements which a court must observe before imposing certain sentences and makes two new disposals (a combination order and a custody probation order) available to the court. The Order restates with some amendments, provision made in the Probation Act (Northern Ireland) 1950 and Part III of the Treatment of Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1976. The Order further makes provision with respect to mentally disordered offenders and to release on licence of sex offenders.

items.title: The Suckler Cow Premium (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1969 in consequence of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the Order”). The Order makes provision in relation to sentencing and the court’s power in relation to the treatment of offenders.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Disclosure) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for the practice and procedure to be followed in magistrates' courts in relation to—

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996) (Confidentiality) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for the practice and procedure to be followed in magistrates' courts in relation to—

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Advance Notice of Expert Evidence) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for mutual disclosure of expert evidence between parties to proceedings for the summary trial of an offence where the person charged with the offence pleads not guilty.

items.title: The Genetically Modified Organisms (Deliberate Release and Risk Assessment) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement as respects Northern Ireland Commission Directive 97/35/EC (O.J. No. L169, 27.6.97, p. 72) adapting to technical progress for the second time Council Directive 90/220/EEC on the deliberate release into the environment of genetically modified organisms, by amending the Genetically Modified Organisms (Deliberate Release) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (S.R. 1994 No. 144) (the 1994 Regulations).

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Domestic Proceedings) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Domestic Proceedings) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996 to take account of amendments to the Magistrates' Courts (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 [S.I. 1981/1675 (N.I. 26)] and the Domestic Proceedings (Northern Ireland) Order 1980 [S.I. 1980/563 (N.I. 5)] effected by Articles 7 and 8 of the Family Law (Northern Ireland) Order 1993 [S.I. 1993/1576 (N.I. 6)].

items.title: Prohibition of Traffic (Causeway End Road, Unclassified No. 41, Lisburn) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order prohibits the use of Causeway End Road, Unclassified No. 41, Lisburn, from its junction with Knockmore Road, Route A520, to its junction with Causeway End Park, Unclassified No. 7007, by motor vehicles.

items.title: The Beef Bones Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to carcases, bones, bone-in beef, other food and ingredients, which are derived from bovine animals aged over six months at slaughter.

items.title: The Social Security (Amendment) (New Deal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”), the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 3) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980—

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make miscellaneous amendments to regulations concerning personal and occupational pension schemes to—

items.title: Company Accounts (Disclosure of Directors' Emoluments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend provisions in Part I of Schedule 6 to the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 (“the 1986 Order”) relating to the disclosure of directors' emoluments or other benefits in the notes to a company’s annual accounts in respect of any financial year. They also make amendments to Article 254 of the 1986 Order, as amended by the Companies (1986 Order) (Accounts of Small and Medium-sized Companies and Minor Accounting Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (S.R. 1997 No. 436).

items.title: Pharmaceutical Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pharmaceutical Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) by making provision for a chemist’s name to remain on the pharmaceutical list following his death where the business is being carried on by his representatives in accordance with the Medicines Act 1968.

items.title: The Zoonoses Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Article 7 of the Diseases of Animals (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”) empowers the Department, with a view to reducing the risk to human health from any disease carried in animals or poultry, to make an Order designating any disease which, in its opinion, constitutes such a risk and to apply any provision of the 1981 Order to that disease.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order:

items.title: Specified Risk Material Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order revokes the Specified Bovine Material Order (Northern Ireland) 1997 and the Heads of Sheep and Goats Order (Northern Ireland) 1996. This Order amends and replaces the provisions of those Orders prohibiting the use of specified bovine material in feedingstuffs and cosmetic, pharmaceutical and medical products. The controls on the use of specified bovine material in food, and the provisions relating to the removal of specified bovine material from cattle and its destruction are contained in the Specified Risk Material Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997.

items.title: The Specified Risk Material Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations control specified risk material, being material which may contain the agent containing bovine spongiform encephalopathy, in respect of food. The Regulations continue in force provisions in respect of food up to now contained in the Heads of Sheep and Goats (Treatment and Disposal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Specified Bovine Material (Treatment and Disposal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997. The Regulations implement in part—

items.title: Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces with amendments the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Order (Northern Ireland) 1995, the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (Amendment) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996. The principal changes of substance are as follows:—

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends paragraph 6 of Schedule 8B to the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 by reducing from £0·72 to £0·69 the fee which qualifying lenders pay for the purpose of defraying administrative expenses incurred by the Department of Health and Social Services in making payments in respect of mortgage interest direct to those lenders.

items.title: Construction (Use of Explosives) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Protected Rights) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Protected Rights) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 which are now revoked. They also make provisions which are consequential upon Part IV of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”). In addition to minor and drafting amendments the Regulations make the following changes of substance—

items.title: Road Transport Licensing (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact, with amendments, the Road Transport Licensing (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985, and all subsequent amendments.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order makes Park Way, Route A42, Ballymena, from its junction with Thomas Street Roundabout, Route A42, to its junction with Chapel Corner Roundabout, Route A42, a restricted road thereby imposing a 30 miles per hour speed limit on that length of road.

items.title: Rates (Making and Levying of Different Rates) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: A new valuation list comes into effect on 1st April 1997 for the purposes of Part III of the Rates (N.I.) Order 1977. That new valuation list contains general revaluations of all properties in Northern Ireland other than dwelling houses, private garages and private storage premises. These properties (“domestic properties”) are not subject of the general revaluation.

items.title: County Court (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1981 by the substitution of a new Order 48 providing for the procedure to be followed in connection with applications to the county court under the Licensing (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 and the Registration of Clubs (Northern Ireland) Order 1996. The associated Forms have also been revised and certain new Forms have been included.

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Licensing) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules provide for the procedure to be followed in connection with applications to a magistrates' court under the Licensing (Northern Ireland) Order 1996. The Magistrates' Courts (Licensing) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1990 are revoked.

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Registration of Clubs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules make provision for the procedure to be followed in courts of summary jurisdiction in relation to applications under the Registration of Clubs (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: Level Crossing (Meigh) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Meigh railway level crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Lurgan (Bells Row)) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of remotely operated manually controlled barriers in lieu of gates at Lurgan (Bells Row) railway level crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Lurgan (Lake Street)) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of remotely operated manually controlled barriers in lieu of gates at Lurgan (Lake Street) railway level crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Poyntzpass) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of remotely operated manually controlled barriers in lieu of gates at Poyntzpass railway level crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: The Social Security (Disability Living Allowance and Attendance Allowance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) (Transitional) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations allow employers to start the procedures for obtaining a contracting-out certificate under the Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 on or after 1st January 1997. They do this by enabling the Department to treat a notice of intended election to contract out, or an election itself, given or made between 1st January 1997 and 5th April 1997, as if it had been given or made on or after 6th April 1997.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) 1997
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) 1997 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Tribunal (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Tribunal Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Business Tenancies (Notices) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms of notices to be used under the Business Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/725 (N.I. 5)) (“the Order”). They also revoke the Business Tenancies (Notices) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1964 (S.R. & O. (N.I.) 1964 No. 215) (the “previous Regulations”) which prescribed the forms of notices to be used under the Business Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 1964 (1964 c. 36 (N.I.)) (“the Act of 1964”). That Act is repealed by the Order.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend (subject to transitional savings) the Rules governing the procedure of the Lands Tribunal for Northern Ireland, namely, the Lands Tribunal Rules (Northern Ireland) 1976 (hereafter referred to as “the Lands Tribunal Rules”), largely consequential on the repeal and re-enactment with amendments (and without Part II on compensation for improvements) of the Business Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 1964 (1964 c. 36 (N.I .)) by the Business Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/725 (N.I. 5)) (hereafter referred to as “the Act of 1964” and “the Order”, respectively). These Rules come into operation on 1st April 1997, as does the Order.

items.title: The Business Tenancies (1996 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st April 1997 the Business Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: Licensing (Register of Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations prescribe the form of the register of licences for the sale of intoxicating liquor by retail, kept by each clerk of petty sessions, for premises situated in the petty sessions district for which he acts.

items.title: Registration of Clubs (Required Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations revoke and replace the Registration of Clubs (Required Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988.

items.title: Registration of Clubs (Display of Notice) Regulation (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations specify the manner in which a notice of application for the grant of registration of a club must be displayed at the premises for which the grant is sought.

items.title: Registration of Clubs (Certificate of Registration) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This version of the Registration of Clubs (Certificate of Registration) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Assistance for Minor Works to Dwellings (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 4 of the Assistance for Minor Works to Dwellings Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 by adding income-based jobseekers allowance under Part II of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 to the list of qualifying benefits for minor works assistance grant.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Indexation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the indexation requirements contained in Article 51(2) and (3) of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) to apply to pension rights which have been transferred into an occupational pension scheme.

items.title: Rates (Industrial Hereditaments) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: Schedule 14 to the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 provides for the apportionment of the net annual value of a mixed-use hereditament between its use for industrial purposes and its use for other purposes. It further provides that where the overall net annual value of a mixed hereditament does not exceed £300, the hereditament will be treatment as if it were wholly used for industrial purposes. In such a case no apportionment need by carried out.

items.title: Valuation for Rating (Decapitalisation Rate) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations specify the percentage rate to be applied in relation to the notional cost of construction or provision or to the capital value of a hereditament where such a hereditament is valued by reference to the notional cost of constructing or providing it or to its capital value.

items.title: Valuation (Telecommunications) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide that specified property used for the monitoring, processing or transmission of communications signals for the provision of telecommunications services, occupied, or, if unoccupied, owned by British Telecommunications plc, Mercury Communications Limited or Cabletel (Northern Ireland) Limited which would, apart from these Regulations be more than one hereditament shall be treated as one hereditament.

items.title: Valuation for Rating (Docks) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This version of the Valuation for Rating (Docks) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Valuation for Rating (Plant and Machinery) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order substitutes a new paragraph 3 in Part III of Schedule 12 to the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977. It amends the classes of plant and machinery which can be valued for rating purposes to reflect the recommendations contained in the Report of the Expert Advisory Committee chaired by Mr. Derek Wood QC (Cm 2170). The committee reviewed the rating of plant and machinery with a view to updating and harmonising it throughout the United Kingdom.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order makes the road and lengths of road specified in the Schedule restricted roads, thereby imposing a speed limit of 30 miles per hour on those roads.

items.title: The Prison and Young Offenders Centre (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison and Young Offenders Centre Rules (Northern Ireland) 1995 (the principal rules).

items.title: Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) by replacing Schedules 1, 2 and 4 (regulation 8), and amending Schedule 5 to those Regulations to take into account changes in Community legislation since those Regulations were passed (regulation 9).

items.title: Conservation of Scallops Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations lay down certain restrictions on fishing for scallops in the waters within British fishery limits which are adjacent to Northern Ireland.

items.title: Parking Places on Roads (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Parking Places on Roads Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the 1993 Order”).

items.title: Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 so as to:

items.title: Legal Advice and Assistance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981 so as to substitute a new scale of contributions payable for legal advice and assistance under Article 7(2) of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: Legal Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 so as to:

items.title: Licensing (Requirements for Accommodation for Guests) (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These regulations revoke the Licensing (Requirements for Accommodation for Guests) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Scheme Administration) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the administration of occupational pension schemes under the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Mixed Benefit Contracted-out Schemes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations enable certain schemes which provide both salary related and money purchase benefits to be contracted-out under Part III of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 (c. 49) (“the Pension Schemes Act”) after 6th April 1997.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Payments to Employers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations concern the requirements which must be satisfied before the employer may receive a payment or distribution of assets from an occupational pension scheme. The requirements are applicable only to exempt approved trust schemes.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Modification of Schemes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision relating to the modification of occupational pension schemes under Articles 67 and 68 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) and related civil penalties under Article 10 of the Order.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations replace the Occupational Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) which are now revoked. The Regulations supplement changes introduced by the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”). They also consolidate and amend those provisions of the 1986 Regulations which are carried forward in these Regulations.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Independent Trustee) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997
items.description: These Regulations concern trust schemes and the duties imposed by Articles 22 to 26 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) on an insolvency practitioner or the official receiver acting in relation to an employer to ensure that there is an independent trustee and to appoint one if there is not. They replace the Occupational Pension Schemes (Independent Trustee) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) which are, subject to transitional provisions, now revoked. Regulations 1 to 6 consolidate and amend the 1990 Regulations. In addition to minor and drafting amendments the Regulations make the following changes of substance—

items.title: Pensions Measure 1997 (Appointed Day Instrument 1997)
Southampton International Boat Show Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend section 60 of the Hampshire Act 1983 to permit closure of Mayflower Park in Southampton or the restriction of public access to it for 10 rather than 9 consecutive days each year for the purposes of the Southampton International Boat Show; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Imperial College Act 1997
items.description: An Act to unite the Charing Cross and Westminster Medical School and the Royal Postgraduate Medical School with the Imperial College of Science, Technology and Medicine; to transfer all rights, properties, assets and liabilities from those medical schools to the said College; to make provision with respect to the merger of the National Heart and Lung Institute with the said College; and for connected purposes.

items.title: King’s College London Act 1997
items.description: An Act to unite the United Medical and Dental Schools of Guy’s and St. Thomas’s Hospitals and King’s College London; to transfer all rights, properties and liabilities from the Schools to the College; and for connected and other purposes.

items.title: Scottish Agricultural College Order Confirmation Act 1997
items.description: An Act to confirm a Provisional Order under the Private Legislation Procedure (Scotland) Act 1936, relating to Scottish Agricultural College.

items.title: Horserace Totalisator Board Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to confer power on the Horserace Totalisator Board to receive or negotiate bets made otherwise than by way of pool betting.

items.title: Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to special controls in respect of hazardous substances with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Planning (Consequential Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for repeals, consequential amendments, transitional matters and savings in connection with the consolidation of enactments in the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997, the Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) (Scotland) Act 1997 and the Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Scotland) Act 1997 (including provisions to give effect to recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission).

items.title: Civil Procedure Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the law about civil procedure in England and Wales; and for connected purposes.

items.title: United Nations Personnel Act 1997
items.description: An Act to enable effect to be given to certain provisions of the Convention on the Safety of United Nations and Associated Personnel adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations on 9th December 1994.

items.title: National Heritage Act 1997
items.description: An Act to extend the powers of the Trustees of the National Heritage Memorial Fund.

items.title: Consolidated Fund Act 1997 (repealed 15.7.1999)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1996 and 1997.

items.title: Finance Act 1997
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with Finance.

items.title: Criminal Evidence (Amendment) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision extending the categories of persons from whom non-intimate body samples may be taken without consent under Part V of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984; and to add a further time limit to those operating for the purposes of section 63A(4)(a) of that Act.

items.title: Policyholders Protection Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the Policyholders Protection Act 1975; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Pharmacists (Fitness to Practise) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision about finding registered pharmaceutical chemists unfit to practise due to ill health; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Land Registration Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Land Registration Act 1925.

items.title: British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to provide for the acquisition of British citizenship by certain British nationals in Hong Kong.

items.title: Knives Act 1997
items.description: An Act to create new criminal offences in relation to the possession or marketing of, and publications relating to, knives; to confer powers on the police to stop and search people or vehicles for knives and other offensive weapons and to seize items found; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Architects Act 1997
items.description: An Act to consolidate the enactments relating to architects.

items.title: Lieutenancies Act 1997
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to the lieutenancies in Great Britain.

items.title: Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Act 1979 and the enactments amending it.

items.title: Justices of the Peace Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Justices of the Peace Act 1979 and provisions of Part IV of the Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994.

items.title: Transfer of Crofting Estates (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to enable the Secretary of State to dispose of his crofting estates and certain other property of his in the crofting counties to approved crofting bodies; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to re-state, with amendments, Part IV of the Social Security Administration Act 1992.

items.title: Merchant Shipping and Maritime Security Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Merchant Shipping Act 1995; to extend the powers of fire authorities to use fire brigades and equipment at sea; to make further provision about the protection of wrecks; to amend Part III of the Aviation and Maritime Security Act 1990; to make provision about piracy; to provide for the continuing application to the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund of section 1 of the International Organisations Act 1968; to make provision about the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Local Government and Rating Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make further provision about non-domestic rating; to make further provision about parishes and parish councils; to confer additional powers on parish councils and community councils; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sea Fisheries (Shellfish) (Amendment) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provisions for fisheries for lobsters and other crustaceans.

items.title: Police (Property) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to property in the possession of the police.

items.title: Appropriation Act 1997 (repealed 15.7.1999)
items.description: Apply a sum out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending on 31st March 1998; to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament; and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: Building Societies Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Building Societies Act 1986; to make provision for amalgamating the Building Societies Investor Protection Board and the Deposit Protection Board into a single board and the Building Societies Investor Protection Fund and the Deposit Protection Fund into a single fund; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Confiscation of Alcohol (Young Persons) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to permit the confiscation of intoxicating liquor held by or for use by young persons in public and certain other places; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Contract (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to reform the law of Scotland relating to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence to prove an additional term of a contract or unilateral voluntary obligation, to the supersession of a contract by a deed executed in implement of it and to the obtaining of damages for breach of contract of sale; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Scottish Legal Services Ombudsman and Commissioner for Local Administration in Scotland Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make further provision about the Scottish legal services ombudsman; to alter the jurisdiction of the Commissioner for Local Administration in Scotland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Flood Prevention and Land Drainage (Scotland) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the Flood Prevention (Scotland) Act 1961 in relation to flood prevention measures to be taken by local authorities; to repeal section 11(2) of the Land Drainage (Scotland) Act 1930 and section 8(2) of the Land Drainage (Scotland) Act 1941; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Welsh Development Agency Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the financial limit in section 18(3) of the Welsh Development Agency Act 1975.

items.title: Prisons (Alcohol Testing) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to enable prisoners in England and Wales, and other persons to whom provisions of the Prison Act 1952 are applied by section 43 of that Act, to be tested for alcohol.

items.title: Sexual Offences (Protected Material) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for regulating access by defendants and others to certain categories of material disclosed by the prosecution or by the Criminal Cases Review Commission in connection with proceedings relating to certain sexual and other offences.

items.title: Telecommunications (Fraud) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Telecommunications Act 1984 to make further provision for the prevention of fraud in connection with use of a telecommunication system.

items.title: Protection from Harassment Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for protecting persons from harassment and similar conduct.

items.title: Building Societies (Distributions) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the law in respect of distribution of assets on the take-over or conversion of a building society.

items.title: Police (Health and Safety) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision about the health, safety and welfare at work of members of police forces, special constables, other persons having the powers or privileges of a constable, and police cadets; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Crime (Sentences) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to the treatment of offenders; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Education Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to education in schools and further education in England and Wales; to make provision for the supervision of the awarding of external academic and vocational qualifications in England, Wales and Northern Ireland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Police (Insurance of Voluntary Assistants) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to provide for the insurance by police authorities and the Receiver for the Metropolitan Police District of persons providing voluntary assistance for police purposes.

items.title: National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to provide for new arrangements in relation to the provision within the national health service of medical, dental, pharmaceutical and other services; to make provision about medical lists and vacancies and the sale of medical practices; to make provision about the expenditure of Health Authorities and Health Boards; to make provision about ophthalmic services; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Social Security Administration (Fraud) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to social security offences and to make other amendments of the law relating to the administration of social security.

items.title: Crime and Punishment (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision as respects Scotland in relation to criminal appeals, the disposal of offenders, criminal procedure, evidence in criminal proceedings, the treatment and early release of prisoners, offences committed by newly released prisoners, criminal legal assistance, the police, confiscation of alcohol from persons under 18, sex offenders and the payment by the Lord Advocate of grants for the provision of forensic medical services; to enable courts in England and Wales and Northern Ireland to remit offenders to courts in Scotland in certain circumstances; to make amendments consequential upon the provisions of this Act to the law in other parts of the United Kingdom; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Public Entertainments Licences (Drug Misuse) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the law about public entertainments licences relating to places at or near which controlled drugs are supplied or used and for connected purposes.

items.title: Firearms (Amendment) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Firearms Acts 1968 to 1992; to make provision in relation to the licensing and regulation of pistol clubs; to make further provision for regulating the possession of, and transactions relating to, firearms and ammunition; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Police Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for the National Criminal Intelligence Service and the National Crime Squad; to make provision about entry on and interference with property and with wireless telegraphy in the course of the prevention or detection of serious crime; to make provision for the Police Information Technology Organisation; to provide for the issue of certificates about criminal records; to make provision about the administration and organisation of the police; to repeal certain enactments about rehabilitation of offenders; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sex Offenders Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to require the notification of information to the police by persons who have committed certain sexual offences; to make provision with respect to the commission of certain sexual acts outside the United Kingdom; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Police and Firemen’s Pensions Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Police Pensions Act 1976 and the Fire Services Act 1947 so as to make provision in respect of transfer values and other lump sum payments and permit police and fire authorities to provide information relating to pension schemes.

items.title: Dangerous Dogs (Amendment) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to amend the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Road Traffic Reduction Act 1997
items.description: An Act to require local authorities to prepare reports relating to the levels of road traffic in their areas; and for related purposes.

items.title: Birds (Registration Charges) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision (including provision having retrospective effect) authorising the Secretary of State to impose charges in respect of registrations effected in accordance with regulations under section 6(2) or 7(1) of the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981.

items.title: National Health Service (Private Finance) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision about the powers of National Health Service trusts to enter into agreements.

items.title: Appropriation (No. 2) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending on 31st March 1998 and to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament.

items.title: Finance (No. 2) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with Finance.

items.title: Education (Schools) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with the ending of the assisted places schemes in England and Wales and in Scotland.

items.title: Local Government (Gaelic Names) (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to enable local authorities in Scotland to take Gaelic names; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Law Officers Act 1997
items.description: An Act to enable functions of the Attorney General and of the Attorney General for Northern Ireland to be exercised by the Solicitor General; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Referendums (Scotland and Wales) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for the holding of a referendum in Scotland on the establishment and tax-varying powers of a Scottish Parliament and a referendum in Wales on the establishment of a Welsh Assembly; and for expenditure in preparation for a Scottish Parliament or a Welsh Assembly.

items.title: Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision for the alteration of salaries payable under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975.

items.title: Local Government Finance (Supplementary Credit Approvals) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to permit account to be taken of the reserved part of capital receipts in determining the amount of a supplementary credit approval to be issued to a local authority; and to substitute a power for the existing duty to specify an amortisation period when issuing a supplementary credit approval to a local authority in respect of expenditure treated by the authority as expenditure for capital purposes.

items.title: Firearms (Amendment) (No. 2) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to extend the class of prohibited weapons under the Firearms Act 1968 to include small-calibre pistols.

items.title: Local Government (Contracts) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision about the powers of local authorities (including probation committees and the Receiver for the Metropolitan Police District) to enter into contracts; to enable expenditure of local authorities making administrative arrangements for magistrates' courts to be treated for some purposes as not being capital expenditure; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Plant Varieties Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision about rights in relation to plant varieties; to make provision about the Plant Varieties and Seeds Tribunal; to extend the time limit for institution of proceedings for contravention of seeds regulations; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consolidated Fund (No. 2) Act 1997 (repealed 15.7.1999)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1998 and 1999.

items.title: Special Immigration Appeals Commission Act 1997
items.description: An Act to establish the Special Immigration Appeals Commission; to make provision with respect to its jurisdiction; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Supreme Court (Offices) Act 1997 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the qualification for appointment as, and tenure of office of, Permanent Secretary to the Lord Chancellor and Clerk of the Crown in Chancery.

items.title: Northern Ireland Arms Decommissioning Act 1997
items.description: An Act to make provision connected with Northern Ireland about the decommissioning of firearms, ammunition and explosives; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to town and country planning in Scotland with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) (Scotland) Act 1997
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to special controls in respect of buildings and areas of special architectural or historic interest with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: The Education (Recognised Bodies) Order 1997
items.description: This Order lists all those bodies which appear to the Secretary of State to be recognised bodies within section 214(2)(a) or (b) of the Education Reform Act 1988. These are universities, colleges or other bodies which are authorised by Royal Charter or by or under Act of Parliament to grant degrees and other bodies for the time being permitted by the aforementioned bodies to act on their behalf in the granting of degrees. An award granted by such a body is not an award of a kind referred to in section 214(1), which makes it an offence to grant, offer to grant, or issue any invitation relating to certain unrecognised degrees and awards.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations replace, with amendments, the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 1990, the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993 and 1994 and the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1992.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Amendment) Order 1997
The Misuse of Drugs (Supply to Addicts) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations made under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 revoke that part of the Misuse of Drugs (Notification of and Supply to Addicts) Regulations 1973 (S.I. 1973/799; “the 1973 Regulations”) dealing with notification of addicts and re-enact that part of those regulations dealing with supply to addicts with drafting amendments only. Doctors will no longer be required to send to the Home Office particulars of persons whom they consider to be addicted to the controlled drugs specified in the Schedule. The prohibition on doctors supplying or prescribing cocaine, diamorphine (commonly known as heroin) and dipipanone for such persons except under licence of the Secretary of State or in certain cases for medical treatment is re-enacted.

items.title: The Kingston and District Community National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1997
The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Order 1995 (“the principal Order”), which conferred functions on rent officers in connection with housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy and required them to make determinations and redeterminations in respect of tenancies of dwellings.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (Subsidy) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision for the calculation of housing benefit and council tax benefit subsidy payable under the Social Security Administration Act 1992 to authorities administering housing benefit or council tax benefit.

items.title: The Broadcasting Act 1996 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force as from 1st April 1997 those provisions of the Broadcasting Act 1996 relating to restricted services, the Gaelic Broadcasting Fund and the establishment of the Broadcasting Standards Commission.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1331), as amended by the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1464) (as so amended, “the 1995 Regulations”), which provide for fees to be paid in relation to wireless telegraphy licences granted under section 1 of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 (c. 54). In relation to the 1995 Regulations, these Regulations provide for:

items.title: The Dual-Use and Related Goods (Export Control) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1997
The Export of Goods (Control)(Amendment No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes two additions to entry ML7.a in Part III of Schedule 1 to the Export of Goods Control Order 1994.

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity for Work and Severe Disablement Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1997
The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) Order 1997
The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
The London Priority Route (Amendment) Order 1997
The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The National Health Service (Fund-Holding Practices) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the National Health Service (Fund-holding Practices) (Scotland) Regulations 1993 (S.I 1993/488), the National Health Service (Fund-holding Practices) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1369), the National Health Service (Fund-holding Practices) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1571) and the National Health Service (Fund-holding Practices) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/748).

items.title: The Finance Act 1996, section 197, (Appointed Day) Order 1997
The Air Passenger Duty and Other Indirect Taxes (Interest Rate) Regulations 1997

The Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996 (Code of Practice) (No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996 (Code of Practice) (No. 1) Order 1997.

items.title: The Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which applies in Great Britain, revokes and replaces the Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1994 (“the 1994 Order”).
