items.title: The Extradition (Aviation Security) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences described in sections 2 and 3 of the Aviation Security Act 1982 (c. 36) (damaging or endangering the safety of aircraft) and section 1 of the Aviation and Maritime Security Act 1990 (c. 31) (endangering safety at aerodromes), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States Parties to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Civil Aviation, signed at Montreal on 23rd September 1971 and to the Protocol for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts of Violence at Airports Serving International Civil Aviation, signed at Montreal on 24th February 1988.

items.title: The Extradition (Designated Commonwealth Countries) Order 1991 (Amendment) Order 1997
The Extradition (Drug Trafficking) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences within the meaning of the Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1994 (c. 37) and the Proceeds of Crime (Scotland) Act 1995 (c. 43), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention against Illicit Traffic in Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances, signed at Vienna on 20th December 1988.

items.title: The Extradition (Hijacking) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 (c. 33) so as to make extraditable offences under section 1 of the Aviation Security Act 1982 (c. 36) (hijacking), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Seizure of Aircraft, signed at The Hague on 16th December 1970.

items.title: The Extradition (Internationally Protected Persons) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences within the meaning of the Internationally Protected Persons Act 1978, attempts to commit such offences and participation in such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention on the Prevention of Crimes Against Internationally Protected Persons, including Diplomatic Agents, signed at New York on 14th December 1973.

items.title: The Extradition (Protection of Nuclear Material)Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable the offences described in the Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (c. 18.), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to States Parties to the Convention on the Physical Protection of Nuclear Material, opened for signature at New York and Vienna on 3rd March 1980.

items.title: The Extradition (Safety of Maritime Navigation)Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences described in sections 9, 10 and 11 to 13 of the Aviation and Maritime Security Act 1990 (c. 31) (offences likely to endanger ships and fixed platforms), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States Parties to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Maritime Navigation, signed at Rome on 10th March 1988 and to the Protocol for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Fixed Platforms.

items.title: The Extradition (Taking of Hostages) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 (c. 33) so as to make extraditable offences under section 1 of the Taking of Hostages Act 1982 (c. 28) (hostage-taking), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention against the Taking of Hostages, signed at New York on 18th December 1979.

items.title: The Extradition (Tokyo Convention) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 (c. 33) so as to make extraditable offences under section 1 of the Tokyo Convention Act 1967 (c. 52) (offences on board aircraft), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Seizure of Aircraft, signed at The Hague on 16th December 1970.

items.title: The Extradition (Torture) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable the offence of torture described in section 134 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (c. 33), attempts to commit such an offence and participation in such offences, in the case of certain States Parties to the Convention Against Torture and other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations on 10th December 1984.

items.title: The Court Funds (Amendment) Rules 1997
The Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (Guernsey) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides that section 14 (duty to keep public register of information) of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”), as substituted by section 147 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”), section 21 (offences–penalties etc.) of the 1985 Act, as amended by section 146(1) and (6) of the 1990 Act, and paragraph 3 of Schedule 2 (powers in relation to vessels, aircraft etc.) to the 1985 Act, as amended by section 146(1) and (8) of the 1990 Act, shall extend to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the amendments to the Schedule to the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (Guernsey) Order 1987 provided for (in particular, these amendments have the effect that sub-paragraphs (3) to (5) of paragraph 3 which, by virtue of that Schedule, do not currently extend to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, shall now so extend).

items.title: The Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (Jersey) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides that section 14 (duty to keep public register of information) of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”), as substituted by section 147 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”), and paragraph 3 of Schedule 2 (powers in relation to vessels, aircraft etc.) to the 1985 Act, as amended by section 146(1) and (8) of the 1990 Act, shall extend to the Bailiwick of Jersey with the amendments to the Schedule to the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (Jersey) Order 1987 provided for (in particular, these amendments have the effect that sub-paragraphs (3) to (5) of paragraph 3 which, by virtue of that Schedule, do not currently extend to the Bailiwick of Jersey, shall now so extend).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Salvage Convention) (Jersey) Order 1997
The Transfer of Prisoners (Isle of Man) (No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides, subject to modifications and a supplementary and transitional provision, for the extension to the Isle of Man of provisions of Schedule 1 to the Crime (Sentences) Act 1997 (which relates to the transfer of prisoners within the British Islands) and for the application in relation to the Isle of Man of those provisions as they have effect in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Transfer of Prisoners (Restricted Transfers) (Channel Islands and Isle of Man) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision, pursuant to paragraph 14 of Schedule 1 to the Crime (Sentences) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”), in relation to transfers of prisoners within the British Islands which are “restricted transfers” as defined in paragraph 6 of Schedule 1 to the Act. The persons who may be subject to such transfers are those who have been remanded in custody in connection with an offence, who are serving a sentence of imprisonment in connection with an offence or who are undergoing, or are about to undergo, supervision in the community in connection with an offence.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income)(Argentina) Order 1997
items.description: The Convention with Argentina and amending Protocol are set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Social Security (United States of America) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 so as to give effect to the Supplementary Agreement on social security (which is set out in Schedule 1 to this Order) made between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the United States of America. The Supplementary Agreement amends the Agreement on social security set out in Schedule 1 to the Social Security (United States of America) Order 1984 to take into account changes in United Kingdom legislation, in particular as relates to incapacity benefit.

items.title: The Visiting Forces (Designation) Order 1997
items.description: This Order designates Albania, Bulgaria, the Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Romania, the Slovak Republic, Slovenia and Sweden as countries to which the provisions of the Visiting Forces Act 1952 apply. The Order partially implements the obligations imposed on the United Kingdom by the agreement, dated 19 June 1995, made between Member States of the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) and those other States which have accepted the invitation to take part in the Partnership for Peace (Miscellaneous No. 12 (1996), Cm 3237).

items.title: The Land Registration Fees Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which supersedes the Land Registration Fees Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) and takes effect on 1st April 1997, reduces overall the fees payable in relation to applications made and services provided under the Land Registration Acts 1925 to 1986 and the Rules made under the Land Registration Act 1925 (“the Act”). It does so by:

items.title: The National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997(Commencement No. 1) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”).

items.title: The Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Amendment Order 1997 (revoked)
The Registration of Births, Still-Births and Deaths (Prescription of Errors) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the errors which a district registrar may, in addition to clerical errors, correct in an entry in the register of births, still-births or deaths provided that–

items.title: The Lotteries (Gaming Board Fees) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision as to the fees to be payable to the Gaming Board for Great Britain by societies under Schedule 1A to the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976; by local authorities under Schedule 2 to that Act; and by lottery managers for certification under Schedule 2A to that Act. The level at which such fees were set previously by the Lotteries (Gaming Board Fees) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/468) (“the 1996 Order”), revoked by this Order, is indicated in brackets after each relevant figure below.

items.title: The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1997
Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 3) 1997
The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) (No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which is made under section 2(1) of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (“the Act”), refers to a document entitled “School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 1997” (“the Document”) published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office, which is obtainable from book shops of The Stationery Office Limited and through booksellers.

items.title: The Forest of Dean (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes changes to the boundary between the parishes of West Dean and Coleford in the district of Forest of Dean in the county of Gloucestershire and to the boundaries of parish and district wards and county electoral divisions. Provision is also made for changes in the number of councillors of the district council with effect from the ordinary elections in 1999.

items.title: The Social Security (Lone Parents) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814).

items.title: The Birmingham Health Authority (Transfers of Trust Property) Order 1997
items.description: This Order transfers on 22nd August 1997 to the bodies specified in column (1) of the Schedule to the Order trust property held by the Birmingham Health Authority (article 2).

items.title: The Rules of Procedure (Army) (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1997
The Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations 1981, consequentially on the United Kingdom becoming a party to the International Convention on Civil Liability for Oil Pollution Damage 1992.

items.title: The National Health Service (Pilot Schemes: Financial Assistance for Preparatory Work) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations empower Health Authorities in England and Wales and Scottish Health Boards to give financial assistance to persons wishing to participate in a pilot scheme for the provision of personal medical services under the National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997. Payments may be made in respect of work reasonably undertaken in connection with the preparation of proposals for the pilot scheme.

items.title: The A316 Trunk Road (Richmond) Red Route Traffic Order 1997
The Countryside Stewardship (Amendment)Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Countryside Stewardship Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/695 as amended by S.I. 1996/1481 and S.I. 1996/3123) (the “principal Regulations”) which (extending to England, including the Isles of Scilly) supplement Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 on agricultural methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside (OJ No. L 215, 30.7.92, p. 85), as last amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L 288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as itself rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L 259, 2.10.96, p. 7). Certain agreements covered by this Order are also subject to Commission Regulation (EC) No. 746/96 (OJ No. L 102, 25.4.96, p. 19) laying down detailed rules for the application of the Council Regulation 2078/92, which Commissin Regulation has been amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 435/97 (OJ No. L 67, 7.3.97, p. 2.).

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1997
items.description: This Order (which extends to England and Wales and Scotland) increases the maximum amounts permitted to be taken by way of the sale of chances and as the aggregated amount of value of prizes on any one determination of winners in gaming for prizes at licensed club premises under section 21 of the Gaming Act 1968 (article 2(a)). It also increases the maximum amount of any money prize which may be distributed or offered (article 2(b)).

items.title: The Firemen’s Pensions (Provision of Information) Regulations 1997
The Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Order 1997
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1983 as amended. That Order and the Orders amending it (“the 1983 Order as amended”) are revoked by article 16 and Schedule 6.

items.title: The Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1980 by deleting the definition of “register” and by replacing the references to the “register” in regulation 6 of and Schedule 2 to those Regulations by references to a record, which may be in written or computerised form.

items.title: The Education (School Information) (Wales) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate (with some very minor changes) the Education (School Information) (Wales) Regulations 1994 (as amended), which are revoked. There are no changes of substance. However, following changes made by the Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) Regulations 1997, the information which governing bodies are required to publish under paragraph 16 of Schedule 2 concerning the performance of pupils in GCSE examinations will now cover equivalent achievements in GCSE short course examinations and, in two cases, equivalent GNVQ and NVQ qualifications of the kind referred to in those Regulations.

items.title: The Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) Regulations 1993 by inserting, in regulation 7, a new provision specifying 10 days (not counting any of the days mentioned in regulation 7(7)) as the period within which the appropriate authority is required (under sections 16(4)(c) and 20(4)(c) of the School Inspections Act 1996, as amended by paragraph 7 of Schedule 6 to the Education Act 1997) to take reasonably practicable steps to provide parents with copies of the summary of the inspection report following its receipt by them.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 4) 1997
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Criminal Procedure Rules as follows—

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (English Heritage) Order 1997
The Value Added Tax (Terminal Markets) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Value Added Tax (Terminal Markets) Order 1973 and extends the zero-rating contained in that Order to supplies in the course of dealings on the London Securities and Derivatives Exchange Limited (OMLX), which is now added to the list of terminal markets to which that Order applies.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1997
The County Court (Forms) (Amendment) Rules 1997
The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2740) (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”), the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2890) (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1801) (“the Adjudication Regulations”).

items.title: The Fire Precautions (Workplace) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations (“the Regulations”) give effect in Great Britain to:

items.title: The Council Tax Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) to limit the maximum amount of council tax benefit or alternative maximum council tax benefit to which a claimant may be entitled where the dwelling in which he resides falls within any of the valuation bands F to H to which section 5 or 74 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (c. 14) refers.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Control of Interference from Videosenders) Order 1997
items.description: This Order restricts for the purposes of section 7 of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1967 (c. 72) actions in relation to certain wireless telegraphy apparatus known as videosenders, which transmit in the frequency band 470—854 MHz. The restricted actions are importation (other than for the purpose of re-export from the United Kingdom), manufacture, sale, hire, offer or advertisement for sale or hire and having in one’s custody or control (article 3). The restriction on the importation of videosenders does not apply, however, to imports from a Member State of the European Community or the European Economic Area.

items.title: The West Mercia (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural changes effected by the Hereford and Worcester (Structural, Boundary and Electoral Changes) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1867) and the Shropshire (District of The Wrekin) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1866). The first-mentioned Order abolished the existing county of Hereford and Worcester and replaced it with a new county of Herefordshire (without a county council) and a new county of Worcestershire (with a county council). The second-mentioned Order removed the District of The Wrekin from the County of Shropshire.

items.title: The Cheshire (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Cheshire (Boroughs of Halton and Warrington) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1863). That Order removed the Boroughs of Halton and Warrington from the County of Cheshire.

items.title: The Cambridgeshire (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Cambridgeshire (City of Peterborough) (Structural, Boundary and Electoral Changes) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1878). That Order removed the City of Peterborough from the County of Cambridgeshire.

items.title: The Essex (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Essex (Boroughs of Colchester, Southend-on-Sea and Thurrock and District of Tendring) (Structural, Boundary and Electoral Changes) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1875). That Order removed the Boroughs of Southend-on-Sea and Thurrock from the County of Essex.

items.title: The Thames Valley (Police Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes transitional provision relating to the proceedings of the Thames Valley police authority in consequence of the abolition of Berkshire County Council on 1st April 1998 under the Berkshire (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1879).

items.title: The Devon and Cornwall (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Devon (City of Plymouth and Borough of Torbay) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1865). That Order removed the City of Plymouth and Borough of Torbay from the County of Devon.

items.title: The Nottinghamshire (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Nottinghamshire (City of Nottingham) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1877). That Order removed the City of Nottingham from the County of Nottinghamshire.

items.title: The Housing Act 1996 (Commencement No. 11 and Savings) Order 1997
The Leasehold Valuation Tribunals (Fees) Order 1997
The Leasehold Valuation Tribunals (Service Charges, Insurance or Appointment of Managers Applications) Order 1997
The Rent Assessment Committee (England and Wales) (Leasehold Valuation Tribunal) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Lancashire (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Lancashire (Boroughs of Blackburn and Blackpool) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1868). That Order removed the Boroughs of Blackburn (now called Blackburn with Darwen) and Blackpool from the County of Lancashire.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Technical Services) Order 1997
items.description: The Order describes technical services for the purposes of section 24(3)(b) of the Broadcasting Act 1996 (“the Act”). Technical services must be defined in order for them to be distinguished from digital additional services, which will be licensed by the Independent Television Commission under section 25 of the Act.

items.title: The Kent (Police Area and Authority) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the structural change effected by the Kent (Borough of Gillingham and City of Rochester upon Medway) (Structural Change) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1876). That Order removed the Borough of Gillingham and the City of Rochester upon Medway from the County of Kent.

items.title: The Savings Contracts (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Savings Certificates (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Savings Certificates (Children’s Bonus Bonds) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The General Medical Council (Legal Assessors) (Amendment) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the General Medical Council (Legal Assessors) Rules 1980, which regulate the functions of legal assessors appointed by the General Medical Council to advise the Professional Conduct Committee, the Health Committee and the Preliminary Proceedings Committee on questions of law arising in proceedings before them.

items.title: The Premium Savings Bonds (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Savings Certificates (Yearly Plan) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The National Savings Stock Register (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 14(1) of the Banking Act (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1997 to correct an error. Regulation 14(1) provided that the acceptance of a deposit involving the issue of shorter term debt securities which were not themselves listed would only be an exempt transaction if a single debt security was issued and the redemption value of each such debt security when issued or transferred was not less than £100,000. The amendment gives effect to the intention that these requirements should apply in respect of the issue of any shorter term debt securities, whether listed or not.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Pollution: Substances Other than Oil) (Intervention) Order 1997
items.description: This Order prescribes substances other than oil, the threat of pollution from which (following a shipping casualty) triggers the Secretary of State’s powers of intervention under section 137 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1995 in accordance with section 138A of that Act.

items.title: The Environmental Assessment (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the further implementation in Scotland of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment. They amend Part II (Planning) of the Environmental Assessment (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (“the Environmental Assessment Regulations”) to apply to appeals against enforcement notices and to allow a developer to check the need for environmental assessment in connection with a proposal which may benefit from permitted development rights. In addition the Regulations remove Part IV (Specific Developments in New Towns) of the Environmental Assessment Regulations because the new town development corporations have been wound up.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Order 1992. It is concerned with the further implementation in Scotland of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Building Standards (Relaxation by Local Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with amendments the Building Standards (Relaxation by Local Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Corn Returns Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, revoke and replace the Corn Returns Regulations 1976, the Corn Returns (Scotland) Regulations 1976, the Corn Returns (Scotland) (Variation) Regulations 1990, and the Corn Returns (Variation) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Control of Pesticides (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Pesticides Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1510), as amended (S.I. 1990/2487 and 1994/3142) (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations have been made for the purpose of controlling pesticides in implementation of Part III of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 and require the advertisement, sale, supply, storage and use in Great Britain of any pesticide product falling within the scope of the principal Regulations to be carried out in accordance with an approval granted by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretary of State (“the Ministers”) in relation to that product and in accordance with consents made by the Ministers (“the consents”). The principal amendments are given below.

items.title: The Fish Health Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with amendments the Fish Health Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/3300) as amended.

items.title: The General Medical Council (Registration (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations) Order of Council 1997
items.description: The Regulations approved by this Order revoke the provision in regulation 9 of the Medical Practitioners Registration (Fees) Regulations 1985 exempting from payment of annual retention fees any person who, not having reached the age of 65 years, satisfied the Registration Committee that he or she was prevented, by lasting physical or mental incapacity, from rendering services as a medical practitioner for remuneration resulting in a profit. In future such persons wishing to avoid payment of such fees will be expected to accept voluntary erasure from the register.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1331, “the 1995 Regulations”, as amended by the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1464) and the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/1006)) by amending the definitions in regulation 3(4) relating to wireless telegraphy licences required for programme making.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Voice Telephony) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 95/62/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the application of open network provision (ONP) to voice telephony (“the Voice Telephony Directive”).

items.title: The Trading Schemes (Exclusion) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The National Health Service Pension Scheme (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations amend the National Health Service Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (the Regulations) by bringing NHS general practitioners within the definition of an “employing authority” and thereby enabling persons employed by them to join the NHS Pension Scheme for England and Wales (though periods of non-NHS work will not count as pensionable service).

items.title: The Ecclesiastical Judges and Legal Officers(Fees) Order 1997
items.description: This Order increases the fees fixed by Table I of the Ecclesiastical Judges and Legal Officers (Fees) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) in relation to faculty proceedings, proceedings for an injunction or a restoration order under section 13(4) and (5) of the Care of Churches and Ecclesiastical Jurisdiction Measure 1991 (including appeals) and proceedings under section 4 of the Care of Cathedrals (Supplementary Provisions) Measure 1994 (including appeals), and the fees fixed by Table III of the 1996 Order for proceedings in respect of ecclesiastical offences under the Ecclesiastical Jurisdiction Measure 1963, including provision for all cases where proceedings have been instituted under that Measure or where a person has been authorised to lay a complaint under the Measure, and appeals.

items.title: The Plant Protection Products (Basic Conditions) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations supplement the Plant Protection Products Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/887) as amended (S.I. 1996/1940) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Great Britain Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market, as amended. The principal Regulations require that plant protection products may not be placed on the market or used within Great Britain unless they have been approved by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretary of State (“the Ministers”) and make provision concerning the process of application for, evaluation and granting of approvals, which may be given subject to conditions, and the packaging and labelling of approved plant protection products.

items.title: The Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1997
items.description: This Order increases the annual fees for diocesan registrars fixed by the Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1996.

items.title: The Parochial Fees Order 1997
items.description: Article 1 and Part I of the Schedule establish a new table of fees payable for certain matters in connection with baptisms, marriages and burials, for the erection of monuments in churchyards and for other miscellaneous matters. The fees are in substitution for fees prescribed by the Parochial Fees Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1994).

items.title: The Family Law Act 1996 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force forthwith section 57 of, and paragraphs 3 and 4 of Schedule 9 to, the Family Law Act 1996.

items.title: The Family Proceedings (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Proceedings Rules 1991 so as to–

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Matrimonial Proceedings etc) (Amendment) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Proceedings Courts (Matrimonial Proceedings etc) Rules 1991 so as to provide for the making of applications under Part IV of the Family Law Act 1996 (Family Homes and Domestic Violence) and for the enforcement of orders made under that Part.

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Children Act 1989) (Amendment) Rules 1997
The Family Law Act 1996 (Part IV)(Allocation of Proceedings) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the allocation of proceedings under Part IV of the Family Law Act 1996 (Family Homes and Domestic Violence) between the High Court, the county courts and the magistrates' courts.

items.title: The Children (Allocation of Proceedings) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Children (Allocation of Proceedings) Order 1991 which provides for the allocation of proceedings between the High Court, the county courts and the magistrates' courts of proceedings relating to children.

items.title: The Family Law Act 1996 (Modifications of Enactments) Order 1997
The Family Proceedings Fees (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1997
The Merchant Shipping (Carriage of Cargoes) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations give effect, in relation to sea-going United Kingdom cargo carrying ships and to other sea-going cargo carrying ships while they are within the United Kingdom or the territorial waters thereof, to the amendments to Chapter VI of the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea 1974 adopted on 23rd May 1991 by the Maritime Safety Committee of the International Maritime Organisation at its fifty-ninth session by Resolution MSC.22(59); and revoke and replace the Merchant Shipping (Grain) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Leeds Teaching Hospitals Special Trustees (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1997
items.description: This Order transfers to the Leeds Teaching Hospitals Special Trustees on 28th February 1997 trust property formerly held by the United Leeds Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, Audit Liaison Unit, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Genetically Modified Organisms (Deliberate Release and Risk Assessment-Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Cattle Identification (Enforcement) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations create offences for breach of—

items.title: The Allocation of Housing (Reasonable and Additional Preference) Regulations 1997
items.description: Part VI of the Housing Act 1996 is concerned with the allocation of housing accommodation by a local housing authority in England and Wales. Section 167 requires that an authority’s scheme for determining priorities in allocating housing gives reasonable preference to a range of persons and additional preference to certain of those persons. These Regulations provide that reasonable preference is also to be given to certain categories of persons who have been homeless, and they widen the category of persons to whom additional preference is to be given.

items.title: The Housing (Prescribed Forms) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the forms prescribed by the Housing (Prescribed Forms) (No. 2) Regulations 1990 following the coming into force of Chapter IV of Part I of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996 (deferred action notices). A deferred action notice is to be served by a local housing authority if they are satisfied that a dwelling-house or house in multiple occupation is unfit for human habitation and are satisfied that serving such a notice is the most satisfactory course of action.

items.title: The Building Regulations (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Building Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2768).

items.title: The Bovines and Bovine Products (Despatch Prohibition and Production Restriction) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations give effect to Commission Decision 96/239/EC on emergency measures to protect against bovine spongiform encephalopathy, as amended by Commission Decision 96/362/EC, in relation to the despatch to third countries and other member States of bovine animals and embryos and meat and other products from bovine animals.

items.title: The Knives Act 1997 (Commencement) (No. 1) Order 1997
The Knives (Forfeited Property) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for the disposal of knives subject to a forfeiture order under section 6 of the Knives Act 1997 (c. 21) by the relevant police authority and for the application of any proceeds of such disposals.

items.title: The Police (Property) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England and Wales only, revoke the Police (Disposal of Property) Regulations 1975. They substantially re-enact the provisions in those regulations relating to sale and disposal of property in the possession of the police to which the Police (Property) Act 1897 (“the 1897 Act”) applies with drafting amendments. The regulations also make provision for the retention of property by the police in certain circumstances following amendment to the 1897 Act by the Police (Property) Act 1997 (c. 30).

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Workskill Courses) Pilot (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for two further pilot schemes which modify certain jobseeking regulations in the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207). One scheme, which applies to persons who are required to attend one of the offices listed in Part I of the Schedule in order to claim jobseeker’s allowance, modifies the regulations on availability, active seeking and sanctions for part-time students (regulations 6 to 10 and 18 to 21). The other scheme, which applies to persons who are required to attend one of the offices listed in Part II of the Schedule, modifies the regulations on availability, active seeking and sanctions for full-time students (regulations 11 to 21). In order to qualify for these concessions, a person must satisfy the conditions set out in regulation 3.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 1 to the Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1992 and are made in consequence of Commission Directives 89/491/EEC, 96/20/EC, 96/44/EC, 95/54/EC, 95/56/EC, 96/37/EC, 96/38/EC, 96/64/EC, 96/36/EC, 95/48/EC, 95/28/EC and Directives 96/69/EC, 96/1/EC and 96/27 which were made by the European Parliament and Council.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Merchant Shipping (Training, Certification and Safe Manning) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations correct deficiencies in the Merchant Shipping (Training and Certification) Regulations 1997 and the Merchant Shipping (Safe Manning, Hours of Work and Watchkeeping) Regulations 1997, which implemented the International Convention on Standards of Training, Certification and Watchkeeping (STCW), 1978, as amended on 7th July 1995.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Provision of Information) Regulations 1997
The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Hackney and Southwark London Borough Councils and Liverpool City Council) Order 1997
The Dartford–Thurrock Crossing Tolls Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st September 1997, fixes the amounts of the tolls to be paid to use the Dartford–Thurrock Crossing.

items.title: The Dartford–Thurrock Crossing (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations increase the charges payable to the crossing operator (in addition to tolls) for the passage of abnormal loads through the tunnels or across the bridge and for removing or assisting broken down vehicles.

items.title: The National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) by bringing registered medical practitioners on the list of a Health Board and partnerships of such practitioners within the definition of an “employing authority” and thereby enabling persons employed by them to assist in the provision of general medical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 to join the NHS Superannuation Scheme for Scotland. Periods of work relating solely or partly to the provision of medical services other than general medical services under the 1978 Act are not included in a member’s pensionable service (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Sex Offenders Act 1997 (Commencement) Order 1997
The Sex Offenders (Certificate of Caution) Order 1997
The Fishing Vessels (Decommissioning) Scheme 1997
items.description: This Scheme provides for the making of grants by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretaries of State respectively concerned with the sea fish industry in Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland, in respect of the decommissioning of vessels registered in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Church of England Pensions Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations relate in part to the past service pension scheme and in part to the funded scheme in respect of future service which is provided for by the Pensions Measure 1997. The Regulations also provide for the Constitution of the Pensions Board and for the term of office of its members.

items.title: The Police Act 1997 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force various provisions of the Police Act 1997 in respect of the National Criminal Intelligence Service (“NCIS”), the National Crime Squad (“NCS”) and the Police Information Technology Organisation (“the Organisation”) on 1st September 1997 and 8th October 1997. The Order also amends various provisions relating to NCIS, NCS and the Organisation (including modification of the application of Part IV of the Act in relation to Northern Ireland) and brings into force sections 91 (1) to (9) and 96, which relate to intrusive surveillance.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for the Core Subjects) (Key Stage 1) (England) (Amendment) Order 1997
The Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 12 Regulations 1997
The Electronic Fingerprinting etc. Device Approval (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: This Order approves the device known as the Digital Biometrics Incorporation (DBI) Tenprinter 1133S, with palmprint option, for the purpose of creating such records as are mentioned in paragraph (d) of section 18(7A) of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (records of a person’s skin on an external part of the body).

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations"). They prescribe forms for the purposes of various provisions of Part I of the Housing Act 1988 relating to assured tenancies and assured agricultural occupancies. The use for those purposes of forms substantially to the same effect as the prescribed forms is authorised by regulation 2.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Christchurch Borough Council) Order 1997
The Energy Efficiency (Refrigerators and Freezers) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations implement European Parliament and Council Directive 96/57/EC on energy efficiency requirements for the household electric refrigerators, freezers and their combinations defined in regulation 3. The Directive specifies minimum standards of energy efficiency (in terms of electricity consumption) which are to be met by such appliances which are placed on the Community market.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which replaces the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1986 as amended, makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and obligations concerning technical measures for the conservation of fishery resources which are contained in Council Regulation (EC) No. 894/97 (OJ No. L132, 23.5.97, p. 1) (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Beef (Marketing Payment) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Transport and Works (Guided Transport Modes) (Amendment) Order 1997
The Inverness Harbour Revision Order 1997
items.description: This Order authorises the Trustees of the Harbour of Inverness to construct a quay wall in Inverness and to continue and maintain the said quay wall; and to carry out subsidiary works.

items.title: The Cromarty Firth Port Authority Harbour Revision Order 1997
The Nursery Education (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Wolverhampton Health Care National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1997
The Finance Act 1997, section 7(10), (Appointed Day) Order 1997
items.description: This Order appoints 15th August 1997 as the day on which section 7 of the Finance Act 1997 comes into force.

items.title: The Plant Passport (Plant Health Fees) (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1997
items.description: This Order prescribes units of production for the assessment of the productive capacity of agricultural land and sets out the amount which is to be regarded as the net annual income from each such unit for the year 12th September 1997 to 11th September 1998 inclusive. This Order supersedes the Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/2163).

items.title: The National Board for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting for England (Constitution and Administration) Amendment Order 1997
The Land Registration (Matrimonial Home Rights) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules, which replace the Land Registration (Matrimonial Homes) Rules 1990:

items.title: The Lands Tribunal (Amendment) Rules 1997
The Education (School Inspection) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the Education (School Inspection) (No. 2) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1986) as amended by the Education (School Inspection) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1993, the Education (School Inspection) (No. 2) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1996 and the Education (School Inspection) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1997. These Regulations also amend the Education (Pupil Referral Units) (Application of Enactments) Regulations 1994 to update the statutory references.

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Music, Ballet and Choir Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Education (Assisted Places) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe arrangements for pupils who are eligible to continue to hold assisted places at independent schools in England and Wales by virtue of section 2 of the Education (Schools) Act 1997, notwithstanding the abolition of the assisted places scheme by section 1 of that Act.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for—

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the introduction in Scotland of compulsory competitive tendering under the Local Government Act 1988 in respect of management of vehicles, housing management, security work, and the following professional services:– legal, construction and property, financial, information technology and personnel.

items.title: The Education (School Leaving Date) Order 1997
The Education (Fees and Awards) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1997, replace the Regulations revoked by regulation 8. The drafting has been substantially altered, with only a few changes of substance. These changes will be noted below.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 1997 (S.I.1997/852) so as to omit amendments to regulation 11 of the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I.1987/1971) which would have had the effect from 6th October 1997 of further restricting the maximum rent on which housing benefit may be paid in the case of single claimants aged 25 but under 60 and to provide with respect to cases to whom the amending Regulations are not to apply.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (SS Castilian) Order 1997
The Treasure Act 1996(Commencement No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force on 24th September 1997, with the exception of one provision, the Treasure Act 1996. The provision in section 11 of the Act was bought into force on 13th March 1997 (S.I. 1997/760 (C. 29)).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) Order 1997 Revocation Order 1997
The Accounts Commission for Scotland (Financial Year) Order 1997
items.description: Section 4 of the Audit (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1996 makes provision for changing the financial year of the Accounts Commission for Scotland so that it will in future end on 31st October, rather than on 31st March as at present. This Order makes provision for the transition to the new system, so that the period from 1st April to 31st October 1997 will constitute a financial year of the Commission.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 precludes a defined authority within the meaning of section 1 of that Act from entering into a works contract or carrying out functional work (or such a proportion of functional work as the Secretary of State may specify) in respect of work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of that Act (a defined activity) unless they have competed for the contract or subjected the work to competition. “Works contract” and “functional work” are defined in section 3 of that Act.

items.title: The Local Authority Accounts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: Regulation 4 of the Local Authority Accounts (Scotland) Regulations 1985 inter alia requires the proper officer of a Scottish local authority to ensure that copies of an abstract of the authority’s accounts for a financial year are submitted to the authority and to the Controller of Audit not later than 31st August in the next financial year. These Regulations amend regulation 4 by substituting “30th June” for “31st August”. The change will first take effect as regards accounts for financial year 1997/98.

items.title: The Local Government (Publication of Performance Information) (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: Section 1(2)(b) of the Local Government Act 1992 has effect in Scotland so as to require local authorities, joint committees and joint boards to publish information as to their standards of performance in a financial year (in accordance with directions by the Accounts Commission for Scotland) no later than 9 months after the end of that year. This Order amends that requirement so that relevant bodies will need to effect publication within 6 months after the end of the financial year. The change will first take effect as regards information pertaining to financial year 1997/98.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Housing Benefit Functions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with modifications the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) Order 1995 and revokes the Orders which amended it. It confers functions on rent officers in connection with housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy and requires rent officers to make determinations and redeterminations relating to a tenancy or licence of a dwelling.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1997
The Veal (Marketing Payment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for payments by the appropriate Minister (defined in regulation 2) to producers in the United Kingdom in pursuance of Article 1 of Council Regulation (EC) No. 2443/96 (OJ No. L333, 21.12.96, p.2),, which permits Member States to make additional payments to producers in the beef and veal sector.

items.title: The Anglian Harbours National Health Service Trust Dissolution Order 1997
The A180 Trunk Road (A180/A1136 Junction Improvement Slip Roads) Order 1997
The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1997
The Education (London Residuary Body) (Suspense Account Properties) Order 1997
items.description: This Order applies in respect of the properties listed in Schedule 2 transferred to councils of inner London boroughs and other bodies as a result of the abolition of the Inner London Education Authority.

items.title: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (Further Payments to Capital Stock) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom to the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development of a further subscription not exceeding ECU 851,750,000 to the increased authorised capital stock of the Bank pursuant to arrangements made with the Bank in accordance with Resolution No. 59 adopted by the Board of Governors of the Bank on 15 April 1996. The Order further provides for the redemption of non-interest-bearing and non-negotiable notes issued by the Secretary of State in payment of the subscription. The Order also provides that any sums which may be received by the Government of the United Kingdom in pursuance of the arrangements relating to the further subscription shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Lord-Lieutenants and Sheriffs) Order 1997
The Offshore Electricity and Noise Regulations 1997
The Rent Officers (Housing Benefit Functions) (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with modifications the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”) and revokes the Orders which amended it. It confers functions on rent officers in connection with housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy and requires rent officers to make determinations and redeterminations relating to a tenancy of a dwelling.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Short Range Devices) (Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Short Range Devices) (Exemption) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1591) as amended by the Wireless Telegraphy (Short Range Devices) (Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/2250) and the Wireless Telegraphy (Short Range Devices) (Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations 1995 S.I. 1995/1081) (“the Principal Regulations”). The Principal Regulations provide for the exemption from the licensing requirements of section 1(1) of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 (c. 54) of various short range devices, formerly known as low power devices, so that it is not necessary to hold a licence to establish, install and use the short range devices to which the Principal Regulations apply.

items.title: The Bedfordshire and Hertfordshire Ambulance and Paramedic Service National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1997
The A205 Trunk Road (Upper Richmond Road West) Red Route (Prescribed Route) Traffic Order 1997
The Housing (Change of Landlord) (Payment of Disposal Cost by Instalments) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1997
The A501 Trunk Road (Camden, Islington and Westminster) Red Route Traffic Order 1997
The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Ealing) Red Route (Prescribed Routes and Turns) Traffic Order 1997
The Nursery Education (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These regulations revoke and replace without substantive change the Nursery Education (Amendment) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/1971) made on 8th August 1997 with respect to nursery education in England. They do so in order to remove the possibility of confusion arising from the existence of another set of Regulations of the same title (S.I. 1997/1954) made on 2nd August 1997 in respect of nursery education in Wales.

items.title: The Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules further amend the Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Rules 1994 (“the principal Rules”).

items.title: The Education (Fees and Awards) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Fees and Awards) (Scotland) Regulations 1997 (the 1997 Regulations), Part II of which permits further and higher education institutions to charge a higher fee to overseas students than to home students. The criteria set by the 1997 Regulations for qualification for the home student fee are 3 years ordinary residence in the United Kingdom and Islands prior to the start of a course, no part of this residence having been wholly or mainly for the purpose of receiving full-time education. These Regulations amend the 1997 Regulations to add a further qualifying condition that the student should also be “settled in the United Kingdom” within the meaning of the Immigration Act 1971. Parts III and IV of the 1997 Regulations allow the rules of eligibility for certain awards to restrict the awards to candidates having a relevant connection with Scotland or England respectively. The relevant connection is a residential qualification similar to that for the home student fee. These Regulations again add the requirement that the candidate should be “settled in the United Kingdom”. Regulation 2 provides that the new condition will not apply to students commencing their course before 1st September 1998.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Key Stage 2) (Wales) Order 1997
items.description: Section 356(1) and (2) of the Education Act 1996 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order such attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements as he considers appropriate for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1997
The Education (National Curriculum) (Key Stage 3 Assessment Arrangements) (Wales) Order 1997
items.description: Section 356(1) and (2) of the Education Act 1996 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order such attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements as he considers appropriate for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Key Stage 1) (Wales) Order 1997
items.description: Section 356(1) and (2) of the Education Act 1996 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order such attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements as he considers appropriate for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route (Cycle Lane) Experimental Traffic Order 1997
The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Ealing) Red Route (Prescribed Turns) Traffic Order 1997
The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1997
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation No. 3 Order 1997
The London North Circular Trunk Road (A406) (East of Finchley High Road, Barnet) (Prohibition Of Use Of Gap In Central Reserve) Order 1997
The City of Westminster (A41 Trunk Road) Red Route (Bus Lanes) Experimental Traffic Order 1997
The Stonehenge Regulations 1997
The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (Trunk Roads) (No. 1) Order 1997
items.description: This Order designates a management agent and a works contractor under the Local Authorities (Goods and Services) Act 1970.

items.title: The Lambeth Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1997
items.description: This Order transfers to the Lambeth Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 19th September 1997 trust property formerly held by the Special Trustees for St. Thomas' Hospital. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Processed Cereal-based Foods and Baby Foods for Infants and Young Children Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 31st March 1999, apply to Great Britain and implement Commission Directive 96/5/EC (OJ No. L49, 28.2.96, p. 17) on processed cereal-based foods and baby foods for infants and young children.

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Order 1984 (“the principal Order”) which specifies classes of medicinal products which can with reasonable safety be sold or supplied otherwise than by or under the supervision of a pharmacist (the general sale list).

items.title: The Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Order 1997 (“the principal Order”) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines (i.e. medicinal products which, subject to exemptions, may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a practitioner). Under the principal Order products are included in a class of such medicines by reason of the substances contained in them, subject to their being excluded in specified circumstances.

items.title: The Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1997
The Allocation of Housing and Homelessness (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the classes of persons prescribed under section 161(3) of the Housing Act 1996 (allocation only to qualifying persons) as not qualifying for the allocation of housing accommodation and the descriptions, for which provision is made under section 185(3) of the 1996 Act (persons from abroad not eligible for housing assistance), of persons to be treated as persons from abroad who are ineligible for housing assistance. In each case, the effect of the amendment is to except from the test of habitual residence in the Common Travel Area a person who left the territory of Montserrat after 1st November 1995 because of the effect on that territory of a volcanic eruption.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 7.57 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Social Security Administration (Fraud) Act 1997 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1997
The Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy) Regulations 1994 to include fire authorities in England and Wales constituted as a result of local government reorganisation as relevant bodies for the purposes of Part III of those Regulations.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Judicial Factors Rules) (Amendment) 1997
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Judicial Factors Rules) 1992 so as to make provision in relation to judicial factors appointed under section 9(5)(a) of the Children (Scotland) Act 1995. It also amends rule 15 of those 1992 Rules so as to enable the Accountant of Court to approve, without further procedure, an application by a factor to encroach on the capital of the ward’s estate where the amount of encroachment does not exceed 5% of the initial capital of that estate.

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1996.

items.title: The Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (Detention by Customs Officers) (Specification of Titles) Order 1997
The General Teaching Council for Scotland (Amendment of Constitution) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 1 to the Teaching Council (Scotland) Act 1965 (which provides for the appointment of members to the General Teaching Council for Scotland) to take account of the transfer of colleges of further education from the control of local authorities to being run by boards of management under the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1997
The Animal By-Products (Identification) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Fresh Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area ) (County of Kent) (Borough of Maidstone) Order 1997
items.description: This Order designates an area consisting of part of the borough of Maidstone as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 and applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area. The Order provides that persons appointed to act as parking adjudicators in London may so act in relation to the designated area and the Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (London) Regulations 1993 are made to apply with modifications to adjudicators so acting. The Order also modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) (No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order (which extends to England and Wales and Scotland) increases the maximum amount which may be charged under section 31 of the Gaming Act 1968 for playing a game once by means of a machine to which Part III of the Gaming Act 1968 applies on licensed or registered premises (article 2). This Order also increases the maximum amount which may be charged for playing a machine once and the maximum amount which may be offered as monetary prizes in amusements by means of machines provided at certain fairs, licensed club premises and other commercial establishments under section 34 of that Act (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Camden) Red Route Experimental (No. 2) Traffic Order 1997
The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1997
items.description: This Order (which extends to England and Wales and Scotland) increases from £30 to £50 the maximum amount permitted to be taken by way of the sale of chances in any one determination of winners in amusements provided at certain fairs and other commercial entertainments under section 16 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 6) 1997
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Criminal Procedure Rules by inserting a new chapter 37A providing for applications to the sheriff under section 7(3) of the Knives Act 1997 for delivery of forfeited property to the true owner and by prescribing a form of petition for that purpose.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 5) 1997
The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) Act 1997 (Commencement) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force on 3rd September 1997 specified provisions of the Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) Act 1997 for the purpose of enabling the Secretary of State to make regulations (article 2(1)).

items.title: The Western Isles Council (Brevig) Harbour Revision Order 1997
items.description: This Order authorises the Western Isles Council as the harbour authority for Brevig harbour on the island of Lewis in the Western Isles Area to construct works to form a new approach to the harbour, to extend an area of reclaimed land and retain an existing reclaimed area of land adjacent to the harbour. The Order also confers on the Council duties and powers in relation to the authorised works.

items.title: The Scottish Seed Potato Development Council (Dissolution) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the winding up and dissolution of the Scottish Seed Potato Development Council (article 3), the preparation of final accounts of the Council (article 4) and the imposition and recovery of charges to meet the Council’s liabilities and the expenses of the winding up (article 5). It further provides for the application of surplus monies and property of the Council for specified purposes (article 6), for the notification of debts or claims due by the Council (articles 7 and 8), and the revocation of the Scottish Seed Potato Development Council Order 1981 and the Scottish Seed Potato Development Council (Amendment) Order 1991 (article 9).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (No. 2) Order 1997
The A2 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1997
The Consumer Credit (Quotations) (Revocation) Regulations 1997
The Northern Ireland Arms Decommissioning Act 1997 (Commencement of Section 7) Order 1997
The Authorised Unit Trusts (Interest Distributions) (Qualifying Investments) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends section 468L of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (authorised unti trusts: interest distributions) by adding to the list of “qualifying investments” n subsection (9) of that section qualifying shares in an open-ended investment company which is incorporated in the United Kingdom. The Order also inserts additional subsections in section 468L containing definitions of “open-ended investment company” and other relevant terms.

items.title: The Unit Trust Schemes and Offshore Funds (Non-qualifying Investments Test) Order 1997
The A41 Trunk Road (Camden) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1997
The A23 Trunk Road (Croydon) Red Route (No. 2) Experimental Traffic Order 1997
The A23 Trunk Road (Croydon) Red Route (Prohibited Turns) (No. 2) Experimental Traffic Order 1997
The Wireless Telegraphy (Network User Stations) (Exemption) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for the exemption from the licensing requirements of section 1(1) of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 (c. 54) of stations (“user stations”) designed or adapted for use by customers of certain telecommunication networks. Accordingly it will not be necessary to hold a licence to establish and use the user stations (regulation 3).

items.title: The Income Tax (Employments) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/744) (“the PAYE Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Qualifications, Curriculum and Assessment Authority for Wales) (Conferment of Functions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order confers on Awdurdod Cymwysterau, Cwricwlwm ac Asesu Cymru or the Qualifications, Curriculum and Assessment Authority for Wales (ACCAC) as from 1st October 1997, certain functions with respect to all external academic and vocational qualifications in Wales except National Vocational Qualifications (NVQs). Those functions are conferred solely on ACCAC. By virtue of section 24(1) and (2) of the Education Act 1997 (the 1997 Act) the same functions in relation to England will be vested in QCA (the Qualifications and Curriculum Authority) and by virtue of section 24(3) of the 1997 Act those functions in relation to Wales would, but for this order, also have become vested in QCA.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Council Tax) (Transitional Reduction) Regulations 1997
items.description: Under Part II of the Local Government Act 1992, where recommendations for changes to local government in England are made by the Local Government Commission, the Secretary of State may make an order (“a reorganisation order”). The Local Government Changes for England (Finance) Regulations 1994 make transitional provision for local authorities subject to a reorganisation order.

items.title: The Building (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Building Standards (Scotland) Regulations 1990 (the “1990 Regulations”) and the Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. The amendments take account of the evolution of building practices in the areas of fire prevention and building ventilation, and give effect to some provisions of Directive 93/76/EEC (to limit carbon dioxide emissions by improving energy efficiency).

items.title: The Pensions Act 1995 (Commencement No. 9) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force section 80(4) of, and Schedule 2, paragraph 12 to the Pensions Act 1995, on 4th February 1997 for regulation making purposes, and on 6th April 1997 for all other purposes. It also brings section 55 into force on 4th February 1997.

items.title: The Race Relations (Complaints to Industrial Tribunals) (Armed Forces) Regulations 1997
The Equal Pay (Complaints to Industrial Tribunals) (Armed Forces) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st October 1997, specify the circumstances in which a person may present a complaint to an industrial tribunal in respect of his service in the armed forces, notwithstanding that he would otherwise be precluded from making such a complaint by section 7A(5) of the Equal Pay Act 1970 (which requires a person to go through the service redress procedures before making a complaint to an industrial tribunal).

items.title: The Sex Discrimination (Complaints to Industrial Tribunals) (Armed Forces) Regulations 1997
The Armed Forces Act 1996 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 1997 sections 20 to 27 of the Armed Forces Act 1996 (which relate to applications for redress of complaints and complaints to industrial tribunals). The amendments made by sections 21 to 25 are subject to the transitional provisions specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Direct Labour Organisations) (East Riding of Yorkshire District Council) Order 1997
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Changes for England (Direct Labour and Service Organisations) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/3167) provided, in respect of local authorities affected by a structural change under the Local Government Act 1992, for a period during which the tendering requirements of Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 were disapplied. The Regulations provided for the period to end 18 months after the date on which the structural change took place. In the case of the council of the district of the East Riding the period thus ends on 30th September 1997. This Order provides, in relation to building work (defined in the Order), for a supplementary period of six months during which the tendering requirements are disapplied.

items.title: The Water Supply Byelaws (Scotland) Extension of Period Order 1997
The County Court (Forms) (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules make a correction in Form N.11B (form of reply to application for accelerated possession under section 21 of the Housing Act 1988). The form was introduced by S.I. 1997/1838 (L.28).

items.title: The Education (Qualifications and Curriculum Authority and Qualifications, Curriculum and Assessment Authority for Wales) (Transfer of Property and Designation of Staff) Order 1997
