The Education (School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions Document 1996 (“the Document”) by uprating the amounts of salary and certain allowances. The following are increased:

items.title: The Community Care (Direct Payments) Act 1996 (Commencement) Order 1997
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 1997 sections 1 to 5 and 7 of the Community Care (Direct Payments) Act 1996 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Enzootic Bovine Leukosis Order 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts the Enzootic Bovine Leukosis Order 1980 (S.I. 1980/79 as amended by S.I. 1995/13).

items.title: The Brucellosis Order 1997
items.description: This Order revokes and substantially re-enacts the Brucellosis (England and Wales) Order 1981 (S.I. 1981/1455 as amended by S.I. 1986/2295 and S.I. 1994/2762). It also applies to Scotland for the first time and revokes and re-enacts the Brucellosis (Scotland) Order 1979 (S.I. 1979/1596 as amended by S.I. 1980/1673, S.I. 1987/135 and S.I. 1994/2770).

items.title: The Isles of Scilly (Direct Payments Act) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides that sections 1 and 2 of the Community Care (Direct Payments) Act 1996 (“the Act”) apply to the Isles of Scilly as if the Council of the Isles of Scilly were a local authority for the purposes of those sections.

items.title: The Treasure Act 1996 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1997
The City of Manchester (Egerton Street Service Bridge Scheme) 1994 Confirmation Instrument 1997
The City of Manchester (Egerton Street West Bridge Scheme) 1994 Confirmation Instrument 1997
The A127 Trunk Road (M25 To Rayleigh Section and Slip Roads) (Detrunking) Order 1997
The Betting, Gaming and Lotteries Act 1963 (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1997
The Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officer’s Charges) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which applies in Northern Ireland, revokes and replaces the Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officer’s Charges) (Northern Ireland) Order 1994 (“the 1994 Order”).

items.title: The Borough of Thurrock (Electoral Changes) Order 1997
The District of the Medway Towns (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
The City of Peterborough (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for boundary and electoral changes in the city of Peterborough (“the city”).

items.title: The Rent Officers (Housing Renewal Grants Functions) Order 1997
The Borough of Halton (Electoral Changes) Order 1997
The District of The Wrekin (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for boundary and electoral changes in the district of The Wrekin (“the district”).

items.title: The Borough of Warrington (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for boundary and electoral changes in the borough of Warrington.

items.title: The Borough of Blackburn (Parishes and Electoral Changes) Order 1997
The Borough of Blackpool (Electoral Changes) Order 1997
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Blackpool. All of the existing wards of the borough are abolished and 22 new wards are created, having the names set out in the Schedule. Each new ward has two councillors. The areas of the new wards are demarcated on the map referred to in article 1(2). Prints of the map may be inspected at all reasonable times at the offices of the Blackpool Borough Council and at the offices of the Secretary of State for the Environment which, until May 1997, are located at 2 Marsham Street, London SW1P 3EB and, thereafter, at 2nd Floor, Ashdown House, 123 Victoria Street, London SW1E 6DE.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Discharge of Liability) Regulations 1997
items.description: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Discharge of Liability) Regulations 1997 (“the Regulations”) consolidate the Occupational Pension Schemes (Discharge of Liability) Regula tions 1985 with minor and drafting amendments and make provision in respect of pensions and accrued rights under a scheme contracted out by virtue of section 9(2B) of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 so far as is attributable to an earner’s service on or after 6th April 1997.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Assignment, Forfeiture, Bankruptcy etc.) Regulations 1997
items.description: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Assignment, Forfeiture, Bankruptcy etc.) Regulations 1997 (“the Regulations”) are the first Regulations to be made under sections 91 to 94 of the Pensions Act 1995 (c. 26).

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend a number of sets of regulations related to the Pensions Act 1995.

items.title: The County Court Fees (Amendment) Order 1997
The Family Proceedings Fees (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Family Proceedings Fees Order 1991 by restoring the provisions governing exemption and remission from court fees to their wording immediately prior to the coming into force of the Family Proceedings Fees (Amendment) Order 1996. It also revokes the relevant articles of the 1996 Order.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) Rules 1997
The Home Energy Efficiency Scheme Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for the making of grants for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons on low income or elderly persons; for the appointment of area agencies to administer the making of grants; and for the appointment of registered installers capable of carrying out the works for which grants may be made. The Regulations revoke and re-enact the Home Energy Efficiency Grants Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/483, as amended) with further amendments, among which are the following:—

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Workskill Courses) Pilot Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for two pilot schemes which modify certain jobseeking regulations in the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207). One scheme, which applies to persons who are required to attend one of the offices listed in Part I of the Schedule in order to claim jobseeker’s allowance, modifies the regulations on availability, active seeking and sanctions for part-time students (regulations 6 to 10 and 18 to 21). The other scheme, which applies to persons who are required to attend one of the offices listed in Part II of the Schedule, modifies the regulations on availability, active seeking and sanctions for full-time students (regulations 11 to 21). In order to qualify for these concessions, a person must satisfy the conditions set out in regulation 3.

items.title: The Social Security (Social Fund and Claims and Payments) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund Maternity and Funeral Expenses (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I.1987/481 as amended) (“the General Regulations”) and the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (S.I.1987/1968 as amended) (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”) in so far as those Regulations relate to claims for payment of maternity and funeral expenses.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No.2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1968) in the following respects:

items.title: The Occupational Pensions Regulatory Authority (Determinations and Review Procedure) Regulations 1997
items.description: The Occupational Pensions Regulatory Authority (“the Authority”) is established under section 1 of the Pensions Act 1995 (c. 26).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 1) (Part I Orders) 1997
The Town and Country Planning (Inquiries Procedure) (Scotland) Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules amend and replace the Town and Country Planning (Inquiries Procedure) (Scotland) Rules 1980, which are revoked, subject to the transitional provisions contained in rule 25(2).

items.title: The Homelessness (Isles of Scilly) Order 1997
items.description: This Order modifies Part VII of the Housing Act 1996 (homelessness) as it relates to the Isles of Scilly.

items.title: The Reserve Forces Appeal Tribunals Rules 1997
items.description: These Rules establish and regulate Reserve Forces Appeal Tribunals. The Tribunals are subject to the supervision of the Council on Tribunals and these Rules are based on their Model Rules. The Tribunals are newly established under Part IX of the Reserve Forces Act 1996 to hear appeals against determinations of applications by reservists or their employers under Part VIII of the 1996 Act. Under Part VIII a person liable to call-out or recall for permanent service, or his employer, may apply for payment if he has suffered, or will suffer, loss of income as a result of that person’s absence on permanent service. Reservists or their employers may also apply for exemption or deferral of liability for call-out or recall.

items.title: The Electricity (Non-Fossil Fuel Sources) (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: This Order imposes on public electricity suppliers in Scotland obligations to make arrangements to secure the availability during specified periods of specified aggregate amounts of generating capacity from non-fossil fuel generating stations of particular descriptions. The arrangements must be made, and evidence of their making must be produced to the Director General of Electricity Supply, before 1st May 1997.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Rail Link (Qualifying Authorities) Order 1997
items.description: This Order specifies the local authorities which are qualifying authorities for the purposes of Schedule 6 to the Channel Tunnel Rail Link Act 1996.

items.title: The National Health Service Pension Scheme and Provision of Information and Administrative Expenses etc. (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (“the Pension Scheme Regulations”) and the National Health Service (Provision of Information and Administrative Expenses etc.) Regulations 1996 (“the Provision of Information Regulations”).

items.title: The Mental Health (Hospital, Guardianship and Consent to Treatment) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Mental Health (Hospital, Guardianship and Consent to Treatment) Regulations 1983 by prescribing forms for continuing the detention in hospital or guardianship of a patient who returns, or is returned, after being absent without leave from hospital or the place where he ought to be for more than 28 days.

items.title: The Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Energy Information (Washing Machines) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Medicines Control Agency Trading Fund (Variation) Order 1997
items.description: This Order varies the Medicines Trading Fund Order 1993 by extending the operations for which the Medicines Control Agency Trading Fund is established to include those relating to good laboratory practice. These operations are carried out in connection with measurs relating to the control of laboratories carrying out tests on chemical products as provided for in Council Directive 87/18/EEC of 18th December 1986 and Council Directive 88/320/EEC of 9th June 1988 and as arising out of the United Kingdom’s Membership of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development.

items.title: The Family Credit and Disability Working Allowance (General) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973) (“the Family Credit Regulations”), the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887) (“the Disability Working Allowance Regulations”) and the Income-related Benefits and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Personal Allowances for Children and Young Persons) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2545) (“the Income-related Benefits Regulations”).

items.title: Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (Continuance) Order 1997
items.description: This Order continues in force, with exceptions, the provisions of Parts I to V and section 27(6)(c) of the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”), as amended, for a period of 12 months from 22nd March 1997. The exceptions are the provisions of Parts III and V of the 1989 Act so far as they have effect in Northern Ireland and relate to proscribed organisations for the purposes of section 30 of the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”) or offences or orders under that section. Separate provision is made for the continuance in force of those provisions under section 62(3) of the 1996 Act by virtue of section 27(11) of the 1989 Act.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Lincolnshire) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) so as to reduce the number of justices on the Committee for the Lincolnshire Area from 21 to 12. The petty sessional division of Lincoln District was formerly represented by 6 justices; the petty sessional divisions of Elloes, Grantham, Spilsby/Skegness, Boston and Gainsborough by 2. The other petty sessional divisions continue to be represented as before by a single justice.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (Greater London Enterprise Limited) Order 1997
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates Greater London Enterprise Limited as a public body under the Local Authorities (Goods and Services) Act 1970.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Tests) Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1995 and the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations 1985 by—

items.title: The War Pensions (Mercantile Marine) (Amendment) Scheme 1997
The Personal Injuries (Civilians) Amendment Scheme 1997
items.description: This Scheme further amends the Personal Injuries (Civilians) Scheme 1983 (S.I. 1983/686) (“the principal Scheme”) which makes provision for the payment of pensions and allowances to or in respect of civilians who were killed or injured during the 1939–45 World War.

items.title: The Bovine Hides Regulations 1997
The Sweeteners in Food (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, come into force on 14th April 1997.

items.title: The Motor Vehicle Tyres (Safety) (Amendment)Regulations 1997
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Corporate Debt Exemption) Order 1997
items.description: This Order extends the range of corporate debt instruments which can be the subject of transactions which fall within Schedule 5 of the Financial Services Act 1986 (c. 60) (“FSA”) and therefore benefit from the exemption granted in section 43 of the FSA. The order replaces the separate categories of investment currently set out in paragraphs 2(2)(a) and (b) of Schedule 5 of the FSA and in Article 2 of the Financial Services Act 1986 (Listed Money Market Institutions and Miscellaneous Exemptions) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/696) with a single category of corporate debt instruments having a maturity date of not more than five years from the date of issue.

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain transactions as ones to which the prohibition on unauthorised deposit-taking imposed by section 3 of the Banking Act 1987 does not apply. They replace the Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1988 as amended. In addition to minor and drafting amendments they make the following changes of substance.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”) which provide a scheme for payments to be made by Health Authorities and NHS trusts by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with sight tests and the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Reference Scheme and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations:—

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Rail Link (Planning Appeals) Regulations 1997
items.description: The Channel Tunnel Rail Link Act 1996 (c. 61) makes provision for the nominated undertaker to make requests for planning approval to relevant local authorities. Schedule 6 to that Act provides that the nominated undertaker may appeal to the Secretaries of State for the Environment and for Transport in certain circumstances against the decision of such an authority.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Rail Link (Fees for Requests for Planning Approval) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision about fees for requests for planning approval made by the undertaker to relevant local authorities pursuant to Schedule 6 to the Channel Tunnel Rail Link Act 1996.

items.title: The Workmen’s Compensation (Supplementation) (Amendment) (No. 2) Scheme 1997
The Pneumoconiosis, Byssinosis and Miscellaneous Diseases Benefit (Amendment) Scheme 1997
The Gas (Extent of Domestic Supply Licences) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the remaining stages in the introduction of competition in the supply of gas through pipes to premises at which the rate of supply is not expected to exceed 2,500 therms a year (“domestic premises”).

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations include amendments to the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) and the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1968).

items.title: The Farm Woodland (Amendment) Scheme 1997
The Farm Woodland Premium Scheme 1997
items.description: This Scheme, which applies to Great Britain provides for the payment of annual grants to abate financial losses incurred in consequence of the conversion of agricultural land (including, in Scotland, common grazings) to use for woodlands. The Scheme complies with Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2080/92 instituting a Community aid scheme for forestry measures in agriculture (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.96).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Prohibition) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles (Prohibition) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Loch Ewe, Isle of Ewe, Wester Ross, Scallops Several Fishery Order 1997
items.description: This Order confers on Mrs Jane Grant for a period of 10 years the right of several fishery for scallops in part of Loch Ewe (article 2). The part of Loch Ewe to which the Order applies is described in Schedule 1. The location of the area is also shown on the definitive map (article 1(2)). A copy of the definitive map is held by the Scottish Office Agriculture, Environment and Fisheries Department, Pentland House, 47 Robb’s Loan, Edinburgh EH14 1TW and by the applicant at No. 2, Isle-Ewe, Aultbea, Ross-shire IV22 2JW. A representation of the definitive map is appended to this Note.

items.title: The Lifts Regulations 1997 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement the European Parliament and Council Directive 95/16/EC (OJ No. L213, 7.9.95, p.1) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to lifts (“the Directive”). They come into force on 1st July 1997 except that regulation 2(1)(b), which relates to the extension of the Directive to the European Economic Area, shall not have effect until the coming into force of the relevant Decision of the EEA Joint Committee. The Regulations revoke, with effect from 1st July 1999, the Electrically, Hydraulically and Oil-Electrically Operated Lifts (Components) (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2748) (regulation 1(2)).

items.title: The West Cheshire National Health Service Trust Dissolution Order 1997
The Wirral and West Cheshire Community National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order establishes the Wirral and West Cheshire Community National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3). It specifies the operational date (the date on which the trust assumes all its functions) and the accounting date of the trust (article 5), and it specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the assets (article 6).

items.title: The Wirral Community Healthcare National Health Service Trust Dissolution Order 1997
The Calderdale Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Order which established the Calderdale Healthcare National Health Service Trust to alter the purpose for which the trust has been established, and to amend its functions. (The trust has, in exercise of its existing functions, assumed responsibility for the ownership and management of hospital accommodation and services provided at Halifax General Hospital, Salterhebble, Halifax HX3 0PW, for the management of community health services provided from Royal Halifax Infirmary, Free School Lane, Halifax, West Yorkshire HX1 2YP and for the ownership of associated premises. These services and accommodation were previously managed by the former Calderdale Health Authority.)

items.title: The Worcester Royal Infirmary National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Order which established the Worcester Royal Infirmary National Health Service Trust to alter the purpose for which the trust has been established, and to amend its functions. (The trust has, in exercise of its existing functions, assumed responsibility for the ownership and management of hospital accommodation and services provided at Worcester Royal Infirmary (Newtown Branch), Newtown Road, Worcester, Worcestershire WR5 1JG, for the management of community health services provided from these premises and for the ownership of associated premises. These services and accommodation were previously managed by the former Worcester and District Health Authority.)

items.title: The Carlisle Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Order which established the Carlisle Hospitals National Health Service Trust to alter the purpose for which the trust has been established, and to amend its functions. (The trust has, in exercise of its existing functions, assumed responsibility for the ownership and management of hospital accommodation and services provided at Cumberland Infirmary, Newtown Road, Carlisle, Cumbria CA2 7HY. These services and accommodation were previously managed by the former East Cumbria Health Authority.)

items.title: The Caribbean Development Bank (Further Payments) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of a sum not exceeding £10,600,000 as a further contribution to the Special Development Fund of the Caribbean Development Bank in accordance with arrangements made with the Bank pursuant to the Resolution entitled “Resolution of Contributors to the Special Development Fund of the Caribbean Development Bank” adopted at the Meeting of the Contributors on 20 October 1995. The Order further provides for the redemption of non-interest-bearing and non-negotiable notes issued by the Secretary of State in payment of the further contribution. The Order also provides that any sums which may be received by the Government of the United Kingdom in pursuance of the arrangements relating to the further contribution shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.

items.title: The African Development Fund (Seventh Replenishment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of a sum not exceeding £70,225,661 as a further subscription to the African Development Fund in accordance with the arrangements made with the Fund pursuant to the resolution entitled “Resolution F/BG/96/04 concerning additions to the Resources of the Fund: Seventh General Replenishment” adopted by the Board of Governors of the Fund on 23rd May 1996 as amended by the resolution entitled “Resolution F/BG/96/09 authorising special subscriptions under the Seventh General Replenishment of the Resources of the Fund” adopted by the Board of Governors on 30th September 1996.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) Regulations 1981 as follows.

items.title: The International Development Association (Interim Trust Fund) Order 1997
items.description: The Overseas Development and Co-operation Act 1980, section 6(2), provides that if the Government of the United Kingdom becomes bound by arrangements for the making of additional payments to the International Development Association, the Secretary of State may, with the approval of the Treasury, provide by order for the payment out of money provided by Parliament of any sums required by him for any of the purposes specified in that subsection.

items.title: The Civil Procedure Act 1997 (Commencement No. 1)Order 1997
The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (East London River Crossing (A13 to A2)) Orders 1988 and 1991, Revocation Order 1997
The Registration of Births and Deaths (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births and Deaths Regulations 1987 and the Registration of Births and Deaths (Welsh Language) Regulations 1987. They make provision for the implementation of the Deregulation (Still-Birth and Deaths Registration) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/2395), which will allow persons registering a death or still-birth to do so by making a declaration before a person other than the registrar for the sub-district where the death or still-birth occurred.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Rushcliffe Borough Council) Order 1997
The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1996 by modifying the provision which determines, for the purpose of interpreting those regulations, when a licence ceases to be in force. They substitute a new provision for regulation 4(3) of the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1997, which is defectively drafted and which would otherwise come into force on 1st April 1997.

items.title: The M4 Motorway (Heathrow Airport Spur Road) (Bus Lane) Regulations 1997
The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (Trunk Roads) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Local Government (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (Trunk Roads) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/342) [“the 1996 Order”].

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (Trunk Roads) (No. 2) Order 1997
items.description: This Order designates the Secretary of State for Transport, the Secretary of State for Wales and a DBFO contractor under the Local Authorities (Goods and Services) Act 1970 (article 3).

items.title: The Superannuation (Admission to Schedule 1 to the Superannuation Act 1972) (No. 3) Order 1997
The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) (“the Council Tax Benefit Regulations”) and the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) (“the Housing Benefit Regula tions”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.2) (Adoption Applications) 1997
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 in relation to applications under the Adoption (Scotland) Act 1978. In particular it makes such provision as is requisite to take account of amendments made to that Act by Part III of, and Schedule 2 to, the Children (Scotland) Act 1995.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 3) (Human Fertilisation and Embryology) 1997
The Medicines (Bal Jivan Chamcho Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment Order 1997
The Welfare Food (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welfare Food Regulations 1996 (“the principal Regula tions”).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Development Procedure) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Development Procedure) Order 1995. It introduces a requirement for a local planning authority to consult the British Waterways Board before the grant of planning permission for development likely to affect specified inland waterways, reservoirs, canal feeder channels, watercourses, let offs and culverts.

items.title: The Amusement Machine Licence Duty (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) (Modification and Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order modifies the effect of the Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) Order 1990.

items.title: The House of Commons Disqualification Order 1997
The Local Authorities (Armorial Bearings) Order 1997

The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (Euro-Mediterranean Agreement establishing an Association between the European Communities and their Member States and the State of Israel) Order 1997 (revoked)
The Falkland Islands Constitution (Amendment) Order 1997
The Local Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends Part I of Schedule 2 to the Local Elections (Northern Ireland) Order 1985 and extends to Northern Ireland only.

items.title: The Local Elections (Variation of Limits of Candidates' Election Expenses) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997
items.description: Article 2 of this Order increases the maximum amount of a candidate’s election expenses at local elections in Northern Ireland. The maximum amount is made up of a fixed sum (expressed in pounds) plus a sum expressed in pence for each entry in the register of electors. Article 3 revokes the Order by which the last increase was made.

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Redbridge) (No.1) Red Route Traffic Order 1996 Experimental Variation Order 1997
The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Amendment) Order 1997
The Social Security (Jamaica) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 so as to give effect to the Convention on social security and Protocol signed on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of Jamaica.

items.title: The Housing (Prescribed Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations provide for the amendment of prescribed forms relating to houses in multiple occupation following the coming into force of the provisions of Part II of the Housing Act 1996 relating to such houses.

items.title: The Driving Standards Agency Trading Fund Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the setting up as from 8.00 am on 1st April 1997 of a fund with public money under the Government Trading Funds Act 1973 (“the 1973 Act”) for the operations of the executive agency of the Department of Transport known as the Driving Standards Agency.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Derwen National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 1997 of the Derwen National Health Service Trust established on 1st December 1993.

items.title: The Pembrokeshire and Derwen National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1997
items.description: This Order establishes the Pembrokeshire and Derwen National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 6).

items.title: The Pembrokeshire National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 1997 of the Pembrokeshire National Health Service Trust established on 16th December 1991.

items.title: The Water Undertakers (Extension of Byelaws) Order 1997
The Representation of the People (Variation of Limits of Candidates’ Election Expenses) Order 1997
items.description: This Order increases the maximum amounts of candidates’ election expenses at parliamentary elections in the United Kingdom (articles 2 to 5), local government elections in Great Britain (article 6) and ward elections (article 7) and elections by liverymen in common hall (article 8) in the City of London.

items.title: The A312 Trunk Road (Hounslow) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1997
The Representation of the People (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Representation of the People Regulations 1986 and extend to England and Wales.

items.title: The Air Navigation (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Criminal Justice and Public Order Act 1994 (Commencement No. 11 and Transitional Provision) Order 1997
The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which revokes the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1996, as amended by the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) (Amendment) Order 1996, makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and other obligations relating to sea fishing set out in Council Regulation (EC) No. 390/97 (OJ No. L66, 6.3.97, p.1) (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation fixes total allowable catches and member States’ quotas for 1997 and lays down certain conditions under which they may be fished.

items.title: The Plant Protection Products (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (ACC Long Distance UK Ltd) Order 1997
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to ACC Long Distance UK Ltd on 18th December 1996 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence in the United Kingdom. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th March 1997. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, ACC Long Distance UK Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when this Order comes into force on 17th April 1997.

items.title: The Education (Fees and Awards) (Scotland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 17th February 1997, consolidate the Regulations revoked by regulation 14, with some modifications. In particular, they introduce an amendment to confer on the spouse of an EEA migrant worker (defined in regulation 2) the right to pay “home rate” as opposed to higher “overseas rate” fees at institutions referred to in Part II of the Regulations. They also prohibit rules of eligibility for certain awards referred to in Parts III and IV from excluding such spouses from eligibility.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Worldcom International Inc) Order 1997
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Worldcom International Inc on 5th February 1997 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence in the United Kingdom. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th March 1997. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Worldcom International Inc will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of the telecommunication systems run under that licence when this Order comes into force on 17th April 1997.

items.title: The Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which replaces and revokes the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1996, provides for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and other obligations relating to sea fishing set out in Council Regulation (EC) No. 391/97 (OJ No. L66, 6.3.97, p.49) and Council Regulation (EC) No. 393/97 (OJ No. L66, 6.3.97, p.61) (“the Community Regulations”). The Community Regulations respectively authorise fishing by vessels of Norway and the Faroe Islands for specified descriptions of fish in certain specified areas within member States’ fishery limits in 1997 and contain requirements concerning fishing quotas and authorised zones, methods of fishing, the holding of licences and observance of licence conditions, the keeping of log books, the making of radio reports and similar matters.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Members of the Forces) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations modify the provisions relating to the jobseeker’s allowance under the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 in their application to persons who are, or are treated as being, serving members of Her Majesty’s Forces.

items.title: The Queen Elizabeth II Conference Centre Trading Fund Order 1997
items.description: This Order provides for the setting up as from 1st April 1997 of a fund with public money under the Government Trading Funds Act 1973 (“the 1973 Act”) for the operations of the Department of the Environment known as The Queen Elizabeth II Conference Centre. It designates the responsible minister as the authorised lender to the fund.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Housing Management) (Exemptions) (Wales) Order 1997
items.description: Under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (Competition), work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by defined authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition) (Housing Management) (Wales) Regulations 1997
items.description: The Regulations make provision under the competitive tendering requirements in Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the Act”) in respect of the defined activity of housing management.

items.title: The Companies Act 1985 (Audit Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Gas (Calculation of Thermal Energy) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gas (Calculation of Thermal Energy) Regulations 1996 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 1997
The Revenue Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1997
The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1997
items.description: This Order fixes for the year 1997-98 the aggregate amount of the housing support grants payable to some local authorities under section 191 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987; lists the councils among whom the grants will be apportioned; and prescribes the method of apportionment among those councils of the general and hostel portions of the aggregate amount.

items.title: The Deregulation (Gaming on Sunday in Scotland) Order 1997
The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations are consequential on the Deregulation (Gaming on Sunday in Scotland) Order 1997 (S.I. 941). That Order, which comes into force on the same day as these Regulations, amends section 18 of the Gaming Act 1968 so that that section will prohibit gaming on Sunday in Scotland between 4am and 2pm. Prior to that amendment the prohibition was between 4am and 7.30pm.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general medical services are provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 7.20 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) Order 1997
The Occupational Pension Schemes (Age-related Payments) Regulations 1997 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations specify further circumstances in which age-related payments are not to be paid (in addition to those contained in regulation 37 of the Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1172)) and require the trustees or managers of an occupational pension scheme to provide affected members with certain relevant information.

items.title: The Deregulation (Betting Licensing) Order 1997
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994, amends various provisions in the Betting, Gaming and Lotteries Act 1963 (“the 1963 Act”) relating to the licensing of betting offices and permits for bookmakers and betting agencies, in order to reduce the burden on businesses operating in the betting industry.

items.title: The Ipswich Port Authority Transfer Scheme 1996 Confirmation Order 1997
items.description: This Order confirms the Ipswich Port Authority Transfer Scheme 1996, as modified by the Secretary of State for Transport.

items.title: The Courses for Drink-Drive Offenders (Experimental Period) Order 1997
The Deregulation (Casinos) Order 1997
The Local Government Pension Scheme (Provision of Information, Administrative Expenses and Restitution) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) and make provision in connection with persons who have been eligible to be members of the Local Government Pension Scheme (“the Scheme”) but have instead made contributions to a personal pension scheme and have suffered loss as a result of a contravention which is actionable under section 62 of the Financial Services Act 1986 (“the affected persons”).

items.title: The Social Security (Adjudication) and Commissioners Procedure and Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1995, the Social Security Commissioners Procedure Regulations 1987 and the Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1992 in so far as they relate to applications for leave to appeal against a tribunal decision.

items.title: The Education (New Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations apply to new grant-maintained schools situated in England established pursuant to proposals published by the Funding Agency for Schools (“the FAS”) under section 211, or by promoters under section 212, of the Education Act 1996 in relation to the financial year commencing on 1st April 1997 (“the financial year in question”). The Regulations do not apply to such schools established in place of an existing independent school or in connection with proposals for the discontinuance of another grant-maintained school. The Regulations do not apply to grant-maintained special schools.

items.title: The Deregulation (Employment in Bars) Order 1997
The Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) Regulations 1978 by removing from the requirements which must be satisfied before a person can be promoted to, or appointed in, the rank of firefighter, the requirements as to maximum age, minimum and maximum height measurements and eyesight. The Regulations also make a number of consequential amendments to the 1978 Regulations.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road, Hounslow) Red Route (Prescribed Routes) Traffic Order 1997
The Non-Domestic Rating (Chargeable Amounts for Small Hereditaments) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Non-Domestic Rating (Chargeable Amounts) Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”) which make provision for the five year period beginning on 1st April 1995 in relation to non-domestic rates under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 as to the chargeable amount for which a ratepayer is liable in certain circumstances.

items.title: The British Gas plc (Rateable Values) (Amendment) Order 1997
The Contracting Out (functions of the Registrar General in relation to authorising re-registration of births) Order 1997
items.description: This Order makes provision to enable the Registrar General for England and Wales to authorise another person to exercise his functions in relation to authorising the re-registration of births to add the name of a child’s father where the parents are not married, or on legitimation of a child.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Investment Advertisements) (Exemptions) Order 1997
items.description: This Order amends certain provisions of the Financial Services (Investment Advertisements) (Exemptions) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/1586) (“the 1996 Order”) which sets out various exemptions from the restrictions on advertising imposed by section 57 of the Financial Services Act 1986 (“the FSA”).

items.title: The Charities (Dormant Accounts) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charities (Dormant Accounts) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Grants for Improvement of School Security (Scotland) Regulations 1997
The Friendly Societies (Insurance Business) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Representation of the People (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Representation of the People (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1986 and extend to Northern Ireland only.

items.title: The Sheep Scab Order 1997
The European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) Regulations 1972.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains—Extension) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lleyn Peninsula) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Clwydian Range) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Preseli) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Ynys Môn) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Radnor) Designation (Amendment) Order 1997
The Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Housing Renewal Grants (Prescribed Form and Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the form, set out in the Schedule to the Housing Renewal Grants (Prescribed Form and Particulars) Regulations 1996, to be used by owner-occupiers and tenants when applying for housing renewal grants under Chapter I of Part I of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996. Most of the amendments are consequential to amendments being made by the Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/977) to the Housing Renewal Grants Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2890, amended by S.I. 1996/3119).  There are also minor and drafting amendments.

items.title: The Representation of the People (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Representation of the People (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) and extend to Scotland only.

items.title: The National Health Service (Indicative Amounts) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the calculation of the basic price of drugs, medicines and listed appliances for the purpose of enabling Health Authorities to compare the actual costs with the indicative amounts for doctors’ practices which they are required to specify under section 18 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. An indicative amount is an amount of money representing the basic price of drugs, medicines and listed appliances which a Health Authority considers it reasonable to expect will be supplied in a financial year pursuant to orders given by or on behalf of a medical practice.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Contract for Work) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations establish a pilot scheme under the Jobseekers Act 1995. The scheme relates to persons who claim jobseeker’s allowance and have been receiving benefit for at least two years and attending an office of the Department for Education and Employment specified in the Schedule.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Project Work Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations apply, with modifications, the pilot scheme established by the Income Support (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1252) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1307) to persons attending offices in further parts of the country. The scheme relates to persons who have been claiming income support, unemployment benefit or jobseeker’s allowance or a combination of benefits and who fulfil the criteria in regulation 3 as to age, the period over which they have been receiving benefit and location of appropriate offices at which they are required to attend (as identified in the Schedule) (“relevant persons”).

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Project Work Pilot Scheme) (No. 2) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations replace, with modifications, the pilot scheme established by the Income Support (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1252) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1307) with a further pilot scheme. The scheme relates to persons who have been claiming income support, unemployment benefit or jobseeker’s allowance or a combination of benefits and who fulfil the criteria in regulation 3 as to age, the period over which they have been receiving benefit and location of appropriate offices at which they are required to attend (as identified in the Schedule) (“relevant persons”).

items.title: The Public Works Loans (Fees) (Amendment)Regulations 1997
The Deregulation (Validity of Civil Preliminaries to Marriage) Order 1997
The Stock Lending and Manufactured Payments (Revocations and Amendments) Regulations 1997
The Income Tax (Manufactured Overseas Dividends) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Manufactured Overseas Dividends) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/2004) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Electricity (Class Exemptions from the Requirement for a Licence) Order 1997
items.description: This Order re-enacts the Electricity (Class Exemptions from the Requirement for a Licence) (No. 2) Order 1995 with amendments. It grants exemption from the requirements of section 4(1)(a) of the Electricity Act 1989 (the “Act”) (which prohibits the generation of electricity without a licence) and section 4(1)(c) of the Act (which prohibits the supply of electricity to premises without a licence) to persons of various classes. The classes of persons who are exempt from the requirement to hold a licence to generate electricity are set out in Schedule 2 and the classes of persons who are exempt from the requirement to hold a licence to supply electricity to premises are set out in Schedule 3.

items.title: The Nitrate Sensitive Areas (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Finance Act 1997, Schedule 10, (Appointed Day) Order 1997
The Manufactured Interest (Tax) Regulations 1997
The Manufactured Dividends (Tax) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations make provision for accounting for tax in relation to manufactured dividends on United Kingdom equities other than manufactured dividends paid by companies resident in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Free Zone (Port of Sheerness) (Substitution of Responsible Authority) Order 1997
The Education (School Inspection) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Education (Grant-maintained and Grant-maintained Special Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1997
items.description: These Regulations replace with modifications the Education (Grant-maintained and Grant-maintained Special Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) which are revoked. The Regulations apply, in relation to the 1997–98 financial year, to grant-maintained schools and grant-maintained special schools situated in England but not to completely new grant-maintained schools. Such schools (i.e. those which are not former maintained schools or which are not established in place of existing independent schools) are subject to the Education (New Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/956). The main changes to the provision made by the revoked Regulations are set out below.

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) (Amendment) Regulations 1997
The Legal Aid (Functions) Order 1997
The Local Authorities (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Wales) Regulations 1997
items.description: Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) requires local authorities (including authorities who are treated as local authorities for this purpose) and development bodies, before undertaking construction or maintenance work through their direct labour organisations (DLOs), to have satisfied certain requirements as respect competitive tendering.
